units.text: Enter ORSINO, CURIO, and other lords; Musicians playing

units.speaker: ORSINO
units.text:
If music be the food of love, play on.
Give me excess of it that, surfeiting,
The appetite may sicken, and so die.
That strain again, it had a dying fall.
O, it came o'er my ear like the sweet sound,
That breathes upon a bank of violets,
Stealing and giving odor. Enough, no more.
'Tis not so sweet now as it was before.
O spirit of love, how quick and fresh art thou,
That, notwithstanding thy capacity
Receiveth as the sea, nought enters there,
Of what validity and pitch soe'er,
But falls into abatement and low price
Even in a minute. So full of shapes is fancy
That it alone is high fantastical.

units.speaker: CURIO
units.text: Will you go hunt, my lord?

units.speaker: ORSINO
units.text: What, Curio?

units.speaker: CURIO
units.text: The hart.

units.speaker: ORSINO
units.text:
Why, so I do, the noblest that I have.
Oh, when mine eyes did see Olivia first,
Methought she purged the air of pestilence.
That instant was I turned into a hart,
And my desires, like fell and cruel hounds,
E'er since pursue me.
Enter VALENTINE

units.speaker: ORSINO
units.text: How now! What news from her?

units.speaker: VALENTINE
units.text:
So please my lord, I might not be admitted,
But from her handmaid do return this answer:
The element itself, till seven years' heat,
Shall not behold her face at ample view,
But like a cloistress, she will veiled walk
And water once a day her chamber round
With eye-offending brine —all this to season
A brother’s dead love, which she would keep fresh
And lasting in her sad remembrance.

units.speaker: ORSINO
units.text:
O, she that hath a heart of that fine frame
To pay this debt of love but to a brother,
How will she love, when the rich golden shaft
Hath killed the flock of all affections else
That live in her, when liver, brain, and heart,
These sovereign thrones, are all supplied, and filled
Her sweet perfections with one self king!
Away before me to sweet beds of flowers.
Love thoughts lie rich when canopied with bowers.
Exeunt
Enter VIOLA, a CAPTAIN, and sailors

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text: What country, friends, is this?

units.speaker: CAPTAIN
units.text: This is Illyria, lady.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text:
And what should I do in Illyria?
My brother he is in Elysium.
Perchance he is not drown’d.—What think you, sailors?

units.speaker: CAPTAIN
units.text: It is perchance that you yourself were saved.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text: O, my poor brother! And so perchance may he be.

units.speaker: CAPTAIN
units.text:
True, madam. And, to comfort you with chance,
Assure yourself, after our ship did split,
When you and those poor number saved with you
Hung on our driving boat, I saw your brother,
Most provident in peril, bind himself,
Courage and hope both teaching him the practice,
To a strong mast that lived upon the sea,
Where, like Arion on the dolphin’s back,
I saw him hold acquaintance with the waves
So long as I could see.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text:
[giving him money]
For saying so, there’s gold.
Mine own escape unfoldeth to my hope,
Whereto thy speech serves for authority,
The like of him. Know’st thou this country?

units.speaker: CAPTAIN
units.text:
Ay, madam, well, for I was bred and born
Not three hours' travel from this very place.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text: Who governs here?

units.speaker: CAPTAIN
units.text:
A noble duke, in nature
As in name.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text: What is his name?

units.speaker: CAPTAIN
units.text: Orsino.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text:
Orsino. I have heard my father name him.
He was a bachelor then.

units.speaker: CAPTAIN
units.text:
And so is now, or was so very late.
For but a month ago I went from hence,
And then ’twas fresh in murmur —as, you know,
What great ones do the less will prattle of—
That he did seek the love of fair Olivia.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text: What’s she?

units.speaker: CAPTAIN
units.text:
A virtuous maid, the daughter of a count
That died some twelvemonth since, then leaving her
In the protection of his son, her brother,
Who shortly also died, for whose dear love,
They say, she hath abjured the company
And sight of men.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text:
Oh, that I served that lady
And might not be delivered to the world,
Till I had made mine own occasion mellow,
What my estate is.

units.speaker: CAPTAIN
units.text:
That were hard to compass,
Because she will admit no kind of suit,
No, not the duke’s.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text:
There is a fair behavior in thee, captain,
And though that nature with a beauteous wall
Doth oft close in pollution, yet of thee
I will believe thou hast a mind that suits
With this thy fair and outward character.
I prithee—and I’ll pay thee bounteously—
Conceal me what I am, and be my aid
For such disguise as haply shall become
The form of my intent . I’ll serve this duke.
Thou shall present me as an eunuch to him.
It may be worth thy pains, for I can sing
And speak to him in many sorts of music
That will allow me very worth his service.
What else may hap to time I will commit.
Only shape thou thy silence to my wit.

units.speaker: CAPTAIN
units.text:
Be you his eunuch, and your mute I’ll be.
When my tongue blabs, then let mine eyes not see.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text: I thank thee. Lead me on.
Exeunt
Enter SIR TOBY BELCH and MARIA

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
What a plague means my niece, to take the death of her
brother thus? I am sure care’s an enemy to life.

units.speaker: MARIA
units.text:
By my troth, Sir Toby, you must come in earlier o'
nights. Your cousin, my lady, takes great exceptions to
your ill hours.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: Why, let her except, before excepted.

units.speaker: MARIA
units.text:
Ay, but you must confine yourself within the modest
limits of order.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
Confine? I’ll confine myself no finer than I am. These
clothes are good enough to drink in, and so be these
boots too. An they be not, let them hang themselves in
their own straps.

units.speaker: MARIA
units.text:
That quaffing and drinking will undo you: I heard my
lady talk of it yesterday, and of a foolish knight that
you brought in one night here to be her wooer.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: Who, Sir Andrew Aguecheek?

units.speaker: MARIA
units.text: Ay, he.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: He’s as tall a man as any’s in Illyria.

units.speaker: MARIA
units.text: What’s that to the purpose?

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: Why, he has three thousand ducats a year.

units.speaker: MARIA
units.text:
Ay, but he’ll have but a year in all these ducats. He’s
a very fool and a prodigal.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
Fie, that you’ll say so! He plays o' the
viol-de-gamboys, and speaks three or four languages word
for word without book, and hath all the good gifts of
nature.

units.speaker: MARIA
units.text:
He hath indeed, almost natural, for besides that he’s a
fool, he’s a great quarreler, and but that he hath the
gift of a coward to allay the gust he hath in
quarreling, ’tis thought among the prudent he would
quickly have the gift of a grave.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
By this hand, they are scoundrels and substractors that
say so of him. Who are they?

units.speaker: MARIA
units.text:
They that add, moreover, he’s drunk nightly in your
company.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
With drinking healths to my niece. I’ll drink to her as
long as there is a passage in my throat and drink in
Illyria. He’s a coward and a coistrel that will not
drink to my niece till his brains turn o' th' toe like a
parish top. What, wench! Castiliano vulgo , for here
comes Sir Andrew Agueface.

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text: Sir Toby Belch! How now, Sir Toby Belch!

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: Sweet Sir Andrew!

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text: [To MARIA] Bless you, fair shrew.

units.speaker: MARIA
units.text: And you too, sir.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: Accost, Sir Andrew, accost.

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text: What’s that?

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: My niece’s chambermaid.

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text: Good Mistress Accost, I desire better acquaintance.

units.speaker: MARIA
units.text: My name is Mary, sir.

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text: Good Mistress Mary Accost—

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
You mistake, knight. “Accost” is front her, board her,
woo her, assail her.

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text:
By my troth, I would not undertake her in this company.
Is that the meaning of “accost”?
Fare you well, gentlemen. [she starts to exit]

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
An thou let part so, Sir Andrew, would thou mightst
never draw sword again.

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text:
An you part so, mistress, I would I might never draw
sword again. Fair lady, do you think you have fools in
hand?

units.speaker: MARIA
units.text: Sir, I have not you by the hand.

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text:
Marry, but you shall have, and here’s my hand.
[he offers her his hand]

units.speaker: MARIA
units.text:
[taking his hand] Now, sir, thought is free. I pray
you, bring your hand to the buttery-bar and let it
drink.

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text: Wherefore, sweetheart? What’s your metaphor?

units.speaker: MARIA
units.text: It’s dry, sir.

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text:
Why, I think so. I am not such an ass, but I can keep
my hand dry. But what’s your jest?

units.speaker: MARIA
units.text: A dry jest, sir.

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text: Are you full of them?

units.speaker: MARIA
units.text:
Ay, sir, I have them at my fingers' ends. Marry, now I
let go your hand, I am barren.
Exit

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
O knight, thou lackest a cup of canary. When did I see
thee so put down?

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text:
Never in your life, I think, unless you see canary put
me down. Methinks sometimes I have no more wit than a
Christian or an ordinary man has. But I am a great eater
of beef, and I believe that does harm to my wit.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: No question.

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text:
An I thought that, I’d forswear it. I’ll ride home
tomorrow,
Sir Toby.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: Pourquoi, my dear knight?

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text:
What is “pourquoi”? Do, or not do? I would I had
bestowed that time in the tongues that I have in
fencing, dancing, and bear-baiting. O, had I but
followed the arts!

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: Then hadst thou had an excellent head of hair.

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text: Why, would that have mended my hair?

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
Past question, for thou seest it will not curl by
nature.

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text: But it becomes me well enough, does ’t not?

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
Excellent. It hangs like flax on a distaff. And I hope
to see a housewife take thee between her legs and spin
it off.

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text:
Faith, I’ll home tomorrow, Sir Toby. Your niece will
not be seen. Or if she be, it’s four to one she’ll none
of me. The count himself here hard by woos her.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
She’ll none o' the count. She’ll not match above her
degree, neither in estate, years, nor wit. I have heard
her swear ’t . Tut, there’s life in ’t, man.

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text:
I’ll stay a month longer. I am a fellow o' th'
strangest mind i' th' world. I delight in masques and
revels sometimes altogether.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: Art thou good at these kickshawses, knight?

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text:
As any man in Illyria, whatsoever he be, under the
degree of my betters. And yet I will not compare with an
old man.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: What is thy excellence in a galliard, knight?

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text: Faith, I can cut a caper.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: And I can cut the mutton to ’t.

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text:
And I think I have the back-trick simply as strong as
any man in Illyria.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
Wherefore are these things hid? Wherefore have these
gifts a curtain before 'em? Are they like to take dust,
like Mistress Mall’s picture? Why dost thou not go to
church in a galliard and come home in a coranto? My very
walk should be a jig. I would not so much as make water
but in a sink-a-pace. What dost thou mean? Is it a
world to hide virtues in? I did think, by the excellent
constitution of thy leg, it was formed under the star of
a galliard.

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text:
Ay, ’tis strong, and it does indifferent well in a
dun-colored stock. Shall we set about some revels?

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: What shall we do else? Were we not born under Taurus?

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text: Taurus! That’s sides and heart.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
No, sir, it is legs and thighs. Let me see the caper.
[SIR
ANDREW dances] Ha, higher! Ha, ha, excellent!
Exeunt
Enter VALENTINE and VIOLA in man’s attire, as Cesario

units.speaker: VALENTINE
units.text:
If the duke continue these favors towards you, Cesario,
you are like to be much advanced. He hath known you but
three days, and already you are no stranger.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text:
You either fear his humor or my negligence, that you
call in question the continuance of his love. Is he
inconstant, sir, in his favors?

units.speaker: VALENTINE
units.text: No, believe me.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text: I thank you. Here comes the count.
Enter ORSINO, CURIO, and attendants

units.speaker: ORSINO
units.text: Who saw Cesario, ho?

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text: On your attendance, my lord, here.

units.speaker: ORSINO
units.text:
[To VIOLA and attendants]
Stand you a while aloof. [To VIOLA] Cesario,
Thou know’st no less but all. I have unclasped
To thee the book even of my secret soul.
Therefore, good youth, address thy gait unto her;
Be not denied access, stand at her doors,
And tell them there thy fixed foot shall grow
Till thou have audience.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text:
Sure, my noble lord,
If she be so abandoned to her sorrow
As it is spoke , she never will admit me.

units.speaker: ORSINO
units.text:
Be clamorous, and leap all civil bounds,
Rather than make unprofited return.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text: Say I do speak with her, my lord, what then?

units.speaker: ORSINO
units.text:
O, then unfold the passion of my love,
Surprise her with discourse of my dear faith:
It shall become thee well to act my woes;
She will attend it better in thy youth
Than in a nuncio’s of more grave aspect.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text: I think not so, my lord.

units.speaker: ORSINO
units.text:
Dear lad, believe it.
For they shall yet belie thy happy years
That say thou art a man. Diana’s lip
Is not more smooth and rubious. Thy small pipe
Is as the maiden’s organ, shrill and sound,
And all is semblative a woman’s part.
I know thy constellation is right apt
For this affair. [To CURIO and attendants]
Some four or five attend him.
All, if you will, for I myself am best
When least in company. [To VIOLA] Prosper well in
this,
And thou shalt live as freely as thy lord,
To call his fortunes thine.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text:
I’ll do my best
To woo your lady— [aside] Yet, a barful strife—
Whoe'er I woo, myself would be his wife.
Exeunt
Enter MARIA and the FOOL

units.speaker: MARIA
units.text:
Nay, either tell me where thou hast been, or I will not
open my lips so wide as a bristle may enter in way of
thy excuse. My lady will hang thee for thy absence.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
Let her hang me. He that is well hanged in this world
needs to fear no colors.

units.speaker: MARIA
units.text: Make that good.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text: He shall see none to fear.

units.speaker: MARIA
units.text:
A good lenten answer. I can tell thee where that saying
was born, of “I fear no colors.”

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text: Where, good Mistress Mary?

units.speaker: MARIA
units.text:
In the wars. And that may you be bold to say in your
foolery.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
Well, God give them wisdom that have it. And those that
are fools, let them use their talents.

units.speaker: MARIA
units.text:
Yet you will be hanged for being so long absent. Or to
be turned away, is not that as good as a hanging to
you?

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
Many a good hanging prevents a bad marriage, and, for
turning away, let summer bear it out.

units.speaker: MARIA
units.text: You are resolute, then?

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text: Not so, neither, but I am resolved on two points.

units.speaker: MARIA
units.text:
That if one break, the other will hold. Or, if both
break, your gaskins fall.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
Apt, in good faith, very apt. Well, go thy way. If Sir
Toby would leave drinking, thou wert as witty a piece of
Eve’s flesh as any in Illyria.

units.speaker: MARIA
units.text:
Peace, you rogue, no more o' that. Here comes my lady.
Make your excuse wisely, you were best.
Exit

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
[aside] Wit, an ’t be thy will, put me into good
fooling! Those wits, that think they have thee, do very
oft prove fools. And I, that am sure I lack thee, may
pass for a wise man. For what says Quinapalus? “Better a
witty fool, than a foolish wit.”
Enter OLIVIA with MALVOLIO with attendants

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text: God bless thee, lady!

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: Take the fool away.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text: Do you not hear, fellows? Take away the lady.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text:
Go to, you’re a dry fool. I’ll no more of you.
Besides, you grow dishonest.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
Two faults, madonna, that drink and good counsel will
amend. For give the dry fool drink, then is the fool not
dry. Bid the dishonest man mend himself. If he mend, he
is no longer dishonest. If he cannot, let the botcher
mend him. Anything that’s mended is but patched. Virtue
that transgresses is but patched with sin, and sin that
amends is but patched with virtue. If that this simple
syllogism will serve, so. If it will not, what remedy?
As there is no true cuckold but calamity, so beauty’s a
flower. The lady bade take away the fool. Therefore, I
say again, take her away.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: Sir, I bade them take away you.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
Misprision in the highest degree! Lady, Cucullus non
facit monachum— that’s as much to say as I wear not
motley in my brain. Good madonna, give me leave to prove
you a fool.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: Can you do it?

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text: Dexterously, good madonna.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: Make your proof.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
I must catechise you for it, madonna. Good my mouse of
virtue, answer me.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text:
Well, sir, for want of other idleness, I’ll bide your
proof.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text: Good madonna, why mournest thou?

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: Good fool, for my brother’s death.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text: I think his soul is in hell, madonna.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: I know his soul is in heaven, fool.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
The more fool, madonna, to mourn for your brother’s
soul being in heaven. Take away the fool, gentlemen.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text:
What think you of this fool, Malvolio? Doth he not
mend?

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text:
Yes, and shall do till the pangs of death shake him.
Infirmity, that decays the wise, doth ever make the
better fool.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
God send you, sir, a speedy infirmity, for the better
increasing your folly! Sir Toby will be sworn that I am
no fox, but he will not pass his word for two pence that
you are no fool.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: How say you to that, Malvolio?

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text:
I marvel your ladyship takes delight in such a barren
rascal. I saw him put down the other day with an
ordinary fool that has no more brain than a stone. Look
you now, he’s out of his guard already. Unless you laugh
and minister occasion to him, he is gagged. I protest I
take these wise men that crow so at these set kind of
fools no better than the fools' zanies.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text:
Oh, you are sick of self-love, Malvolio, and taste with
a distempered appetite. To be generous, guiltless, and
of free disposition is to take those things for
bird-bolts that you deem cannon-bullets. There is no
slander in an allowed fool, though he do nothing but
rail. Nor no railing in a known discreet man, though he
do nothing but reprove.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
Now Mercury endue thee with leasing, for thou speakest
well of fools!
Enter MARIA

units.speaker: MARIA
units.text:
Madam, there is at the gate a young gentleman much
desires to speak with you.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: From the Count Orsino, is it?

units.speaker: MARIA
units.text:
I know not, madam. 'Tis a fair young man, and well
attended.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: Who of my people hold him in delay?

units.speaker: MARIA
units.text: Sir Toby, madam, your kinsman.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text:
Fetch him off, I pray you. He speaks nothing but
madman.
Fie on him!
Exit MARIA

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text:
Go you, Malvolio. If it be a suit from the count, I am
sick, or not at home. What you will, to dismiss it.
Exit MALVOLIO

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text:
Now you see, sir, how your fooling grows old, and
people dislike it.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
Thou hast spoke for us, madonna, as if thy eldest son
should be a fool, whose skull Jove cram with brains,
for—here he comes—one of thy kin has a most weak pia
mater.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text:
By mine honor, half-drunk. What is he at the gate,
cousin?

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: A gentleman.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: A gentleman? What gentleman?

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
'Tis a gentleman here— a plague o' these pickle
herring!
How now, sot!

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text: Good Sir Toby!

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text:
Cousin, cousin, how have you come so early by this
lethargy?

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: Lechery! I defy lechery. There’s one at the gate.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: Ay, marry, what is he?

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
Let him be the devil, an he will, I care not. Give me
faith, say I. Well, it’s all one.
Exit

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: What’s a drunken man like, fool?

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
Like a drowned man, a fool and a madman. One draught
above heat makes him a fool, the second mads him, and a
third drowns him.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text:
Go thou and seek the crowner, and let him sit o' my
coz. For he’s in the third degree of drink, he’s
drowned. Go look after him.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
He is but mad yet, madonna, and the fool shall look to
the madman.
Exit
Enter MALVOLIO

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text:
Madam, yond young fellow swears he will speak with you.
I told him you were sick. He takes on him to understand
so much, and therefore comes to speak with you. I told
him you were asleep. He seems to have a foreknowledge of
that too, and therefore comes to speak with you. What
is to be said to him, lady? He’s fortified against any
denial.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: Tell him he shall not speak with me.

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text:
He's been told so, and he says he’ll stand at your
door like a sheriff’s post, and be the supporter to a
bench, but he’ll speak with you.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: What kind o' man is he?

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text: Why, of mankind.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: What manner of man?

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text:
Of very ill manner. He’ll speak with you, will you or
no.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: Of what personage and years is he?

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text:
Not yet old enough for a man, nor young enough for a
boy, as a squash is before ’tis a peascod, or a codling
when ’tis almost an apple. 'Tis with him in standing
water, between boy and man. He is very well-favored, and
he speaks very shrewishly. One would think his mother’s
milk were scarce out of him.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: Let him approach. Call in my gentlewoman.

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text: Gentlewoman, my lady calls.
Exit
Enter MARIA

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text:
Give me my veil. Come, throw it o'er my face. [OLIVIA
puts on a veil] We’ll once more hear Orsino’s embassy.
Enter VIOLA, with attendants

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text: The honorable lady of the house, which is she?

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: Speak to me. I shall answer for her. Your will?

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text:
Most radiant, exquisite and unmatchable beauty—I pray
you, tell me if this be the lady of the house, for I
never saw her. I would be loath to cast away my speech,
for besides that it is excellently well penned, I have
taken great pains to con it. Good beauties, let me
sustain no scorn. I am very comptible, even to the least
sinister usage.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: Whence came you, sir?

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text:
I can say little more than I have studied, and that
question’s out of my part. Good gentle one, give me
modest assurance if you be the lady of the house, that I
may proceed in my speech.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: Are you a comedian?

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text:
No, my profound heart. And yet, by the very fangs of
malice I swear, I am not that I play. Are you the lady
of the house?

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: If I do not usurp myself, I am.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text:
Most certain, if you are she, you do usurp yourself,
for what is yours to bestow is not yours to reserve. But
this is from my commission. I will on with my speech in
your praise and then show you the heart of my message.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text:
Come to what is important in ’t. I forgive you the
praise.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text:
Alas, I took great pains to study it, and ’tis
poetical.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text:
It is the more like to be feigned. I pray you, keep it
in. I heard you were saucy at my gates and allowed your
approach rather to wonder at you than to hear you. If
you be not mad, be gone. If you have reason, be brief.
'Tis not that time of moon with me to make one in so
skipping a dialogue.

units.speaker: MARIA
units.text: Will you hoist sail, sir? Here lies your way.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text:
No, good swabber, I am to hull here a little longer.
Some mollification for your giant, sweet lady.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: Tell me your mind.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text: I am a messenger.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text:
Sure, you have some hideous matter to deliver, when the
courtesy of it is so fearful. Speak your office.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text:
It alone concerns your ear. I bring no overture of war,
no taxation of homage. I hold the olive in my hand . My
words are as full of peace as matter.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: Yet you began rudely. What are you? What would you?

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text:
The rudeness that hath appeared in me have I learned
from my entertainment. What I am and what I would are as
secret as maidenhead. To your ears, divinity. To any
other’s, profanation.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: Give us the place alone. We will hear this divinity.
Exeunt MARIA and attendants

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: Now, sir, what is your text?

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text: Most sweet lady—

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text:
A comfortable doctrine, and much may be said of it.
Where lies your text?

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text: In Orsino’s bosom.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: In his bosom? In what chapter of his bosom?

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text: To answer by the method, in the first of his heart.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text:
Oh, I have read it. It is heresy. Have you no more to
say?

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text: Good madam, let me see your face.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text:
Have you any commission from your lord to negotiate
with my face? You are now out of your text. But we will
draw the curtain and show you the picture. Look you,
sir, such a one I was this present. Is ’t not well done?

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text: Excellently done, if God did all.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: 'Tis in grain, sir. 'Twill endure wind and weather.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text:
'Tis beauty truly blent, whose red and white
Nature’s own sweet and cunning hand laid on.
Lady, you are the cruel’st she alive
If you will lead these graces to the grave
And leave the world no copy.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text:
O, sir, I will not be so hard-hearted. I will give out
divers schedules of my beauty. It shall be inventoried,
and every particle and utensil labeled to my will: as,
item, two lips indifferent red; item, two grey eyes,
with lids to them; item, one neck, one chin, and so
forth. Were you sent hither to praise me?

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text:
I see you what you are, you are too proud.
But, if you were the devil, you are fair.
My lord and master loves you. Oh, such love
Could be but recompensed though you were crowned
The nonpareil of beauty.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: How does he love me?

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text:
With adorations, fertile tears,
With groans that thunder love, with sighs of fire.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text:
Your lord does know my mind. I cannot love him.
Yet I suppose him virtuous, know him noble,
Of great estate, of fresh and stainless youth.
In voices well divulged, free, learned, and valiant;
And in dimension and the shape of nature
A gracious person. But yet I cannot love him;
He might have took his answer long ago.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text:
If I did love you in my master’s flame,
With such a suffering, such a deadly life,
In your denial I would find no sense;
I would not understand it.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: Why, what would you?

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text:
Make me a willow cabin at your gate
And call upon my soul within the house.
Write loyal cantons of contemned love
And sing them loud even in the dead of night.
Halloo your name to the reverberate hills
And make the babbling gossip of the air
Cry out “Olivia!” Oh, you should not rest
Between the elements of air and earth,
But you should pity me.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text:
You might do much.
What is your parentage?

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text:
Above my fortunes, yet my state is well.
I am a gentleman.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text:
Get you to your lord.
I cannot love him. Let him send no more—
Unless perchance you come to me again
To tell me how he takes it. Fare you well.
I thank you for your pains. Spend this for me.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text:
I am no fee’d post, lady. Keep your purse.
My master, not myself, lacks recompense.
Love make his heart of flint that you shall love,
And let your fervor, like my master’s, be
Placed in contempt. Farewell, fair cruelty.
Exit

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text:
“What is your parentage?”
“Above my fortunes, yet my state is well.
I am a gentleman.” I’ll be sworn thou art;
Thy tongue, thy face, thy limbs, actions, and spirit,
Do give thee fivefold blazon. Not too fast! Soft, soft!
Unless the master were the man. How now?
Even so quickly may one catch the plague?
Methinks I feel this youth’s perfections
With an invisible and subtle stealth
To creep in at mine eyes. Well, let it be.—
What ho, Malvolio!
Enter MALVOLIO

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text: Here, madam, at your service.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text:
Run after that same peevish messenger,
The county’s man. He left this ring behind him,
Would I or not. Tell him I’ll none of it.
[OLIVIA hands him a ring]
Desire him not to flatter with his lord,
Nor hold him up with hopes. I am not for him.
If that the youth will come this way tomorrow,
I’ll give him reasons for ’t. Hie thee, Malvolio.

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text: Madam, I will.
Exit

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text:
I do I know not what and fear to find
Mine eye too great a flatterer for my mind.
Fate, show thy force. Ourselves we do not owe.
What is decreed must be, and be this so.
Exit
Enter ANTONIO and SEBASTIAN

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text:
Will you stay no longer, nor will you not that I go
with you?

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text:
By your patience, no. My stars shine darkly over me.
The malignancy of my fate might perhaps distemper yours.
Therefore I shall crave of you your leave that I may
bear my evils alone. It were a bad recompense for your
love to lay any of them on you.

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text: Let me yet know of you whither you are bound.

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text:
No, sooth, sir. My determinate voyage is mere
extravagancy. But I perceive in you so excellent a touch
of modesty that you will not extort from me what I am
willing to keep in. Therefore it charges me in manners
the rather to express myself. You must know of me then,
Antonio, my name is Sebastian, which I called Roderigo.
My father was that Sebastian of Messaline, whom I know
you have heard of. He left behind him myself and a
sister, both born in an hour. If the heavens had been
pleased, would we had so ended! But you, sir, altered
that, for some hour before you took me from the breach
of the sea was my sister drowned.

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text: Alas the day!

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text:
A lady, sir, though it was said she much resembled me,
was yet of many accounted beautiful. But though I could
not with such estimable wonder overfar believe that, yet
thus far I will boldly publish her: she bore a mind
that envy could not but call fair. She is drowned
already, sir, with salt water, though I seem to drown
her remembrance again with more.

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text: Pardon me, sir, your bad entertainment.

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text: O good Antonio, forgive me your trouble.

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text:
If you will not murder me for my love, let me be your
servant.

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text:
If you will not undo what you have done—that is, kill
him whom you have recovered—desire it not. Fare you well
at once. My bosom is full of kindness, and I am yet so
near the manners of my mother, that upon the least
occasion more mine eyes will tell tales of me. I am
bound to the Count Orsino’s court. Farewell.
Exit

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text:
The gentleness of all the gods go with thee!
I have many enemies in Orsino’s court,
Else would I very shortly see thee there.
But, come what may, I do adore thee so
That danger shall seem sport, and I will go.
Exit
Enter VIOLA, MALVOLIO following

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text: Were not you even now with the Countess Olivia?

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text:
Even now, sir. On a moderate pace I have since arrived
but hither.

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text:
She returns this ring to you, sir. You might have saved
me my pains to have taken it away yourself. She adds,
moreover, that you should put your lord into a desperate
assurance she will none of him. And one thing more,
that you be never so hardy to come again in his affairs,
unless it be to report your lord’s taking of this.
Receive it so.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text: She took the ring of me. I’ll none of it.

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text:
Come, sir, you peevishly threw it to her, and her will
is it should be so returned. [he throws down the ring]
If it be worth stooping for, there it lies in your eye.
If not, be it his that finds it.
Exit

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text:
I left no ring with her. What means this lady?
Fortune forbid my outside have not charmed her!
She made good view of me, indeed so much
That sure methought her eyes had lost her tongue,
For she did speak in starts distractedly.
She loves me, sure! The cunning of her passion
Invites me in this churlish messenger.
None of my lord’s ring? Why, he sent her none.
I am the man. If it be so, as ’tis,
Poor lady, she were better love a dream.
Disguise, I see thou art a wickedness,
Wherein the pregnant enemy does much.
How easy is it for the proper false
In women’s waxen hearts to set their forms!
Alas, our frailty is the cause, not we,
For such as we are made of, such we be.
How will this fadge? My master loves her dearly,
And I, poor monster, fond as much on him,
And she, mistaken, seems to dote on me.
What will become of this? As I am man,
My state is desperate for my master’s love.
As I am woman, now, alas the day,
What thriftless sighs shall poor Olivia breathe!
O time, thou must untangle this, not I.
It is too hard a knot for me to untie!
Exit
Enter SIR TOBY BELCH and SIR ANDREW

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
Approach, Sir Andrew. Not to be abed after midnight is
to be up betimes, and diluculo surgere, thou know’st,—

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text:
Nay, my troth, I know not. But I know to be up late is
to be up late.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
A false conclusion. I hate it as an unfilled can. To
be up after midnight and to go to bed then, is early, so
that to go to bed after midnight is to go to bed
betimes. Does not our life consist of the four elements?

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text:
Faith, so they say, but I think it rather consists of
eating and drinking.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
Thou'rt a scholar. Let us therefore eat and drink.
Marian,
I say! A stoup of wine!
Enter FOOL

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text: Here comes the fool, i' faith.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
How now, my hearts! Did you never see the picture of
“We
Three”?

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: Welcome, ass. Now let’s have a catch.

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text:
By my troth, the fool has an excellent breast. I had
rather than forty shillings I had such a leg, and so
sweet a breath to sing, as the fool has.— [To th e FOOL ]
In sooth, thou wast in very gracious fooling last night
when thou spokest of Pigrogromitus, of the Vapians
passing the equinoctial of Queubus. 'Twas very good, i'
faith. I sent thee sixpence for thy leman. Hadst it?

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
I did impeticos thy gratillity, for Malvolio’s nose is
no whipstock. My lady has a white hand, and the
Myrmidons are no bottle-ale houses.

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text:
Excellent! Why, this is the best fooling when all is
done.
Now, a song.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
[giving money to th e FOOL ]
Come on. There is sixpence for you. Let’s have a song.

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text:
[giving money to the FOOL ]
There’s a testril of me too. If one knight give a—

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text: Would you have a love song or a song of good life?

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: A love song, a love song.

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text: Ay, ay. I care not for good life.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
[sings]
O mistress mine, where are you roaming?
O, stay and hear! Your true love’s coming,
That can sing both high and low:
Trip no further, pretty sweeting.
Journeys end in lovers meeting,
Every wise man’s son doth know.

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text: Excellent good, i' faith.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: Good, good.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
[sings]
What is love? 'Tis not hereafter.
Present mirth hath present laughter.
What’s to come is still unsure.
In delay there lies no plenty.
Then come kiss me, sweet and twenty.
Youth’s a stuff will not endure.

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text: A mellifluous voice, as I am true knight.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: A contagious breath.

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text: Very sweet and contagious, i' faith.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
To hear by the nose, it is dulcet in contagion. But
shall we make the welkin dance indeed? Shall we rouse
the night owl in a catch that will draw three souls out
of one weaver? Shall we do that?

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text: An you love me, let’s do ’t. I am dog at a catch.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text: By 'r lady, sir, and some dogs will catch well.

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text: Most certain. Let our catch be “Thou Knave.”

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
“Hold thy peace, thou knave,” knight? I shall be
constrained in ’t to call thee knave, knight.

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text:
'Tis not the first time I have constrained one to call
me
“knave.” Begin, Fool. It begins “Hold thy peace.”

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text: I shall never begin if I hold my peace.

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text: Good, i' faith. Come, begin.
They sing the catch
Enter MARIA

units.speaker: MARIA
units.text:
What a caterwauling do you keep here! If my lady have
not called up her steward Malvolio and bid him turn you
out of doors, never trust me.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
My lady’s a Cataian. We are politicians, Malvolio’s a
Peg- a-Ramsey, and (sings) Three merry men be we. —Am not
I consanguineous? Am I not of her blood? Tillyvally!
“Lady”! ( sings) There dwelt a man in Babylon, lady,
lady!

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text: Beshrew me, the knight’s in admirable fooling.

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text:
Ay, he does well enough if he be disposed, and so do I
too.
He does it with a better grace, but I do it more
natural.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: (sings) O' the twelfth day of December—

units.speaker: MARIA
units.text: For the love o' God, peace!
Enter MALVOLIO

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text:
My masters, are you mad? Or what are you? Have you no
wit, manners, nor honesty but to gabble like tinkers at
this time of night? Do you make an alehouse of my lady’s
house, that you squeak out your coziers' catches
without any mitigation or remorse of voice? Is there no
respect of place, persons, nor time in you?

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: We did keep time, sir, in our catches. Sneck up!

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text:
Sir Toby, I must be round with you. My lady bade me
tell you, that, though she harbors you as her kinsman,
she’s nothing allied to your disorders. If you can
separate yourself and your misdemeanors, you are welcome
to the house. If not, an it would please you to take
leave of her, she is very willing to bid you farewell.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
(sings) Farewell, dear heart, since I must needs be
gone.

units.speaker: MARIA
units.text: Nay, good Sir Toby.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text: ( sings ) His eyes do show his days are almost done.

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text: Is ’t even so?

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: ( sings ) But I will never die.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text: ( sings ) Sir Toby, there you lie.

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text: This is much credit to you.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: (sings) Shall I bid him go?

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text: (sings) What an if you do?

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: (sings) Shall I bid him go, and spare not?

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text: (sings) O no, no, no, no, you dare not.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
Out o' tune, sir. You lie. Art any more than a steward?
Dost thou think, because thou art virtuous, there shall
be no more cakes and ale?

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
Yes, by Saint Anne, and ginger shall be hot i' the
mouth too.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
Thou'rt i' the right. Go, sir, rub your chain with
crumbs. A stoup of wine, Maria!

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text:
Mistress Mary, if you prized my lady’s favor at
anything more than contempt, you would not give means
for this uncivil rule. She shall know of it, by this
hand.
Exit

units.speaker: MARIA
units.text: Go shake your ears!

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text:
'Twere as good a deed as to drink when a man’s
a-hungry, to challenge him the field and then to break
promise with him and make a fool of him.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
Do ’t, knight. I’ll write thee a challenge. Or I’ll
deliver thy indignation to him by word of mouth.

units.speaker: MARIA
units.text:
Sweet Sir Toby, be patient for tonight. Since the youth
of the count’s was today with thy lady, she is much out
of quiet. For Monsieur Malvolio, let me alone with him.
If I do not gull him into a nayword and make him a
common recreation, do not think I have wit enough to lie
straight in my bed. I know I can do it.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: Possess us, possess us, tell us something of him.

units.speaker: MARIA
units.text: Marry, sir, sometimes he is a kind of puritan.

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text: O, if I thought that, I’d beat him like a dog!

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
What, for being a puritan? Thy exquisite reason, dear
knight?

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text:
I have no exquisite reason for ’t, but I have reason
good enough.

units.speaker: MARIA
units.text:
The devil a puritan that he is, or anything constantly,
but a time-pleaser; an affectioned ass that cons state
without book and utters it by great swarths; the best
persuaded of himself, so crammed, as he thinks, with
excellencies, that it is his grounds of faith that all
that look on him love him. And on that vice in him will
my revenge find notable cause to work.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: What wilt thou do?

units.speaker: MARIA
units.text:
I will drop in his way some obscure epistles of love,
wherein by the color of his beard, the shape of his leg,
the manner of his gait, the expressure of his eye,
forehead, and complexion, he shall find himself most
feelingly personated. I can write very like my lady your
niece: on a forgotten matter we can hardly make
distinction of our hands.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: Excellent! I smell a device.

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text: I have ’t in my nose too.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
He shall think, by the letters that thou wilt drop,
that they come from my niece, and that she’s in love
with him.

units.speaker: MARIA
units.text: My purpose is, indeed, a horse of that color.

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text: And your horse now would make him an ass.

units.speaker: MARIA
units.text: Ass, I doubt not.

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text: Oh, ’twill be admirable!

units.speaker: MARIA
units.text:
Sport royal, I warrant you. I know my physic will work
with him. I will plant you two, and let the fool make a
third, where he shall find the letter. Observe his
construction of it. For this night, to bed, and dream on
the event. Farewell.
Exit

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: Good night, Penthesilea.

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text: Before me, she’s a good wench.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
She’s a beagle, true-bred, and one that adores me. What
o' that?

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text: I was adored once too.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
Let’s to bed, knight. Thou hadst need send for more
money.

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text: If I cannot recover your niece, I am a foul way out.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
Send for money, knight. If thou hast her not i' the
end, call me “Cut.”

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text: If I do not, never trust me, take it how you will.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
Come, come, I’ll go burn some sack. 'Tis too late to go
to bed now. Come, knight. Come, knight.
Exeunt
Enter ORSINO, VIOLA, CURIO, and others

units.speaker: ORSINO
units.text:
Give me some music. ( music plays )
Now, good morrow, friends.—
Now, good Cesario, but that piece of song,
That old and antique song we heard last night.
Methought it did relieve my passion much,
More than light airs and recollected terms
Of these most brisk and giddy-paced times:
Come, but one verse.

units.speaker: CURIO
units.text:
He is not here, so please your lordship, that should
sing it.

units.speaker: ORSINO
units.text: Who was it?

units.speaker: CURIO
units.text:
Feste, the jester, my lord, a fool that the lady
Olivia’s father took much delight in. He is about the
house.

units.speaker: ORSINO
units.text: Seek him out, and play the tune the while.
Exit CURIO. Music plays

units.speaker: ORSINO
units.text:
( t o VIOLA ) Come hither, boy. If ever thou shalt love,
In the sweet pangs of it remember me;
For such as I am, all true lovers are,
Unstaid and skittish in all motions else
Save in the constant image of the creature
That is beloved. How dost thou like this tune?

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text:
It gives a very echo to the seat
Where Love is throned.

units.speaker: ORSINO
units.text:
Thou dost speak masterly.
My life upon ’t, young though thou art, thine eye
Hath stay’d upon some favor that it loves.
Hath it not, boy?

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text: A little, by your favor.

units.speaker: ORSINO
units.text: What kind of woman is’t?

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text: Of your complexion.

units.speaker: ORSINO
units.text: She is not worth thee, then. What years, i' faith?

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text: About your years, my lord.

units.speaker: ORSINO
units.text:
Too old by heaven. Let still the woman take
An elder than herself. So wears she to him,
So sways she level in her husband’s heart.
For, boy, however we do praise ourselves,
Our fancies are more giddy and unfirm,
More longing, wavering, sooner lost and worn,
Than women’s are.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text: I think it well, my lord.

units.speaker: ORSINO
units.text:
Then let thy love be younger than thyself,
Or thy affection cannot hold the bent.
For women are as roses, whose fair flower
Being once displayed, doth fall that very hour.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text:
And so they are. Alas, that they are so,
To die even when they to perfection grow!
Enter CURIO and FOOL

units.speaker: ORSINO
units.text:
O, fellow, come, the song we had last night.—
Mark it, Cesario, it is old and plain;
The spinsters and the knitters in the sun
And the free maids that weave their thread with bones
Do use to chant it . It is silly sooth,
And dallies with the innocence of love,
Like the old age.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text: Are you ready, sir?

units.speaker: ORSINO
units.text: Ay; prithee, sing.
Music

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
[sings]
Come away, come away, death,
And in sad cypress let me be laid.
Fly away, fly away breath,
I am slain by a fair cruel maid.
My shroud of white, stuck all with yew,
O, prepare it!
My part of death, no one so true
Did share it.
Not a flower, not a flower sweet
On my black coffin let there be strown.
Not a friend, not a friend greet
My poor corpse, where my bones shall be thrown.
A thousand thousand sighs to save,
Lay me, O, where
Sad true lover never find my grave,
To weep there!

units.speaker: ORSINO
units.text: [giving money] There’s for thy pains.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text: No pains, sir. I take pleasure in singing, sir.

units.speaker: ORSINO
units.text: I’ll pay thy pleasure then.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
Truly, sir, and pleasure will be paid, one time or
another.

units.speaker: ORSINO
units.text: Give me now leave to leave thee.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
Now, the melancholy god protect thee, and the tailor
make thy doublet of changeable taffeta, for thy mind is
a very opal. I would have men of such constancy put to
sea, that their business might be everything and their
intent everywhere, for that’s it that always makes a
good voyage of nothing. Farewell.
Exit

units.speaker: ORSINO
units.text: Let all the rest give place.
CURIO and attendants retire

units.speaker: ORSINO
units.text:
Once more, Cesario,
Get thee to yond same sovereign cruelty.
Tell her my love, more noble than the world,
Prizes not quantity of dirty lands;
The parts that fortune hath bestowed upon her,
Tell her, I hold as giddily as fortune;
But ’tis that miracle and queen of gems
That nature pranks her in attracts my soul.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text: But if she cannot love you, sir?

units.speaker: ORSINO
units.text: I cannot be so answer’d.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text:
Sooth, but you must.
Say that some lady, as perhaps there is,
Hath for your love a great a pang of heart
As you have for Olivia. You cannot love her.
You tell her so. Must she not then be answered?

units.speaker: ORSINO
units.text:
There is no woman’s sides
Can bide the beating of so strong a passion
As love doth give my heart. No woman’s heart
So big, to hold so much. They lack retention.
Alas, their love may be called appetite,
No motion of the liver, but the palate,
That suffer surfeit, cloyment, and revolt;
But mine is all as hungry as the sea,
And can digest as much. Make no compare
Between that love a woman can bear me
And that I owe Olivia.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text: Ay, but I know—

units.speaker: ORSINO
units.text: What dost thou know?

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text:
Too well what love women to men may owe.
In faith, they are as true of heart as we.
My father had a daughter loved a man
As it might be, perhaps, were I a woman,
I should your lordship.

units.speaker: ORSINO
units.text: And what’s her history?

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text:
A blank, my lord. She never told her love,
But let concealment, like a worm i' the bud,
Feed on her damask cheek. She pined in thought,
And with a green and yellow melancholy
She sat like patience on a monument,
Smiling at grief. Was not this love indeed?
We men may say more, swear more, but indeed
Our shows are more than will, for still we prove
Much in our vows, but little in our love.

units.speaker: ORSINO
units.text: But died thy sister of her love, my boy?

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text:
I am all the daughters of my father’s house,
And all the brothers too—and yet I know not.
Sir, shall I to this lady?

units.speaker: ORSINO
units.text:
Ay, that’s the theme.
To her in haste. Give her this jewel. Say
My love can give no place, bide no denay.
[he hands her a jewel]
Exeunt
Enter SIR TOBY BELCH, SIR ANDREW, and FABIAN

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: Come thy ways, Signior Fabian.

units.speaker: FABIAN
units.text:
Nay, I’ll come. If I lose a scruple of this sport, let
me be boiled to death with melancholy.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
Wouldst thou not be glad to have the niggardly rascally
sheep-biter come by some notable shame?

units.speaker: FABIAN
units.text:
I would exult, man. You know, he brought me out o'
favor with my lady about a bear-baiting here.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
To anger him, we’ll have the bear again, and we will
fool him black and blue. Shall we not, Sir Andrew?

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text: An we do not, it is pity of our lives.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: Here comes the little villain.
Enter MARIA

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: How now, my metal of India?

units.speaker: MARIA
units.text:
Get you all three into the boxtree. Malvolio’s coming
down this walk. He has been yonder i' the sun practising
behavior to his own shadow this half hour. Observe him,
for the love of mockery, for I know this letter will
make a contemplative idiot of him. Close, in the name of
jesting!
They hide

units.speaker: MARIA
units.text:
Lie thou there (throwing down a letter), for here comes
the trout that must be caught with tickling.
Exit
Enter MALVOLIO

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text:
'Tis but fortune, all is fortune. Maria once told me
she did affect me, and I have heard herself come thus
near, that, should she fancy, it should be one of my
complexion. Besides, she uses me with a more exalted
respect than anyone else that follows her. What should I
think on ’t?

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: ( aside ) Here’s an overweening rogue!

units.speaker: FABIAN
units.text:
[aside] O, peace! Contemplation makes a rare
turkey-cock of him. How he jets under his advanced
plumes!

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text: ( aside ) 'Slight, I could so beat the rogue!

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: ( aside ) Peace, I say.

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text: To be Count Malvolio!

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: ( aside ) Ah, rogue!

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text: ( aside ) Pistol him, pistol him.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: ( aside ) Peace, peace!

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text:
There is example for ’t. The lady of the Strachy
married the yeoman of the wardrobe.

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text: ( aside ) Fie on him, Jezebel!

units.speaker: FABIAN
units.text:
[aside] O, peace! Now he’s deeply in. Look how
imagination blows him.

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text:
Having been three months married to her, sitting in my
state—

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: ( aside ) Oh, for a stone-bow, to hit him in the eye!

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text:
Calling my officers about me, in my branched velvet
gown, having come from a daybed, where I have left
Olivia sleeping—

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: ( aside ) Fire and brimstone!

units.speaker: FABIAN
units.text: ( aside ) O, peace, peace!

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text:
And then to have the humor of state, and after a demure
travel of regard, telling them I know my place as I
would they should do theirs, to ask for my kinsman Toby—

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: ( aside ) Bolts and shackles!

units.speaker: FABIAN
units.text: ( aside ) O peace, peace, peace! Now, now.

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text:
Seven of my people, with an obedient start, make out
for him. I frown the while, and perchance wind up watch,
or play with my—some rich jewel. Toby approaches,
curtsies there to me—

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: ( aside ) Shall this fellow live?

units.speaker: FABIAN
units.text:
[aside] Though our silence be drawn from us with cars,
yet peace.

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text:
I extend my hand to him thus, quenching my familiar
smile with an austere regard of control—

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
( aside ) And does not Toby take you a blow o' the lips
then?

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text:
Saying, “Cousin Toby, my fortunes having cast me on
your niece give me this prerogative of speech—”

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: ( aside ) What, what?

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text: “You must amend your drunkenness.”

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: ( aside ) Out, scab!

units.speaker: FABIAN
units.text:
( aside ) Nay, patience, or we break the sinews of our
plot.

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text:
“Besides, you waste the treasure of your time with a
foolish knight—”

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text: ( aside ) That’s me, I warrant you.

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text: “One Sir Andrew—”

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text: ( aside ) I knew ’twas I, for many do call me fool.

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text: ( seeing the letter ) What employment have we here?

units.speaker: FABIAN
units.text: ( aside ) Now is the woodcock near the gin.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
[aside] O, peace! And the spirit of humors intimate
reading aloud to him!

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text:
[picking up the letter] By my life, this is my lady’s
hand these be her very C’s, her U’s and her T’s and thus
makes she her great P’s. It is, in contempt of
question, her hand.

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text: ( aside ) Her C’s, her U’s and her T’s. Why that?

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text:
[reads] “To the unknown beloved, this, and my good
wishes”—Her very phrases! By your leave, wax. Soft! And
the impressure her Lucrece, with which she uses to seal.
'Tis my lady. To whom should this be?

units.speaker: FABIAN
units.text: ( aside ) This wins him, liver and all.

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text:
[reads]
“Jove knows I love,
But who?
Lips, do not move;
No man must know.”
“No man must know.” What follows? The numbers altered.
“No man must know.” If this should be thee, Malvolio?

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: ( aside ) Marry, hang thee, brock!

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text:
[reads]
“I may command where I adore,
But silence, like a Lucrece knife,
With bloodless stroke my heart doth gore;
M.O.A.I. doth sway my life.”

units.speaker: FABIAN
units.text: ( aside ) A fustian riddle!

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: ( aside ) Excellent wench, say I.

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text:
“M.O.A.I. doth sway my life.” Nay, but first, let me
see, let me see, let me see.

units.speaker: FABIAN
units.text: ( aside ) What dish o' poison has she dressed him!

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: ( aside ) And with what wing the staniel checks at it!

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text:
“I may command where I adore.” Why, she may command me.
I serve her, she is my lady. Why, this is evident to
any formal capacity. There is no obstruction in this.
And the end—what should that alphabetical position
portend? If I could make that resemble something in
me—Softly! M.O.A.I.—

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: ( aside ) O, ay, make up that.—He is now at a cold scent.

units.speaker: FABIAN
units.text:
[aside] Sowter will cry upon ’t for all this, though
it be as rank as a fox.

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text: “M”—Malvolio. “M”—why, that begins my name.

units.speaker: FABIAN
units.text:
[aside] Did not I say he would work it out? The cur is
excellent at faults.

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text:
“M.” But then there is no consonancy in the sequel.
That suffers under probation. “A” should follow but “O”
does.

units.speaker: FABIAN
units.text: ( aside ) And “O” shall end, I hope.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: ( aside ) Ay, or I’ll cudgel him and make him cry “O!”

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text: And then “I” comes behind.

units.speaker: FABIAN
units.text:
[aside] Ay, an you had any eye behind you, you might
see more detraction at your heels than fortunes before
you.

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text:
“M.O.A.I.” This simulation is not as the former, and
yet to crush this a little, it would bow to me, for
every one of these letters are in my name. Soft, here
follows prose.
[reads]
“If this fall into thy hand, revolve. In my stars I am
above thee, but be not afraid of greatness. Some are
born great, some achieve greatness, and some have
greatness thrust upon 'em. Thy Fates open their hands.
Let thy blood and spirit embrace them. And, to inure
thyself to what thou art like to be, cast thy humble
slough and appear fresh. Be opposite with a kinsman,
surly with servants. Let thy tongue tang arguments of
state. Put thyself into the trick of singularity. She
thus advises thee that sighs for thee. Remember who
commended thy yellow stockings and wished to see thee
ever cross-gartered. I say, remember. Go to, thou art
made, if thou desir’st to be so; if not, let me see thee
a steward still, the fellow of servants, and not worthy
to touch Fortune’s fingers. Farewell. She that would
alter services with thee,
The Fortunate Unhappy”
Daylight and champaign discovers not more. This is
open. I will be proud, I will read politic authors, I
will baffle Sir Toby, I will wash off gross
acquaintance, I will be point- devise the very man. I do
not now fool myself, to let imagination jade me, for
every reason excites to this, that my lady loves me. She
did commend my yellow stockings of late, she did praise
my leg being cross-gartered, and in this she manifests
herself to my love, and with a kind of injunction,
drives me to these habits of her liking. I thank my
stars I am happy. I will be strange, stout, in yellow
stockings, and cross-gartered, even with the swiftness
of putting on. Jove and my stars be praised! Here is yet
a postscript.
[reads]
“Thou canst not choose but know who I am. If thou
entertainest my love, let it appear in thy smiling. Thy
smiles become thee well. Therefore in my presence still
smile, dear my sweet, I prithee.”
Jove, I thank thee! I will smile. I will do everything
that thou wilt have me.
Exit

units.speaker: FABIAN
units.text:
I will not give my part of this sport for a pension of
thousands to be paid from the Sophy.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: I could marry this wench for this device.

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text: So could I too.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: And ask no other dowry with her but such another jest.

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text: Nor I neither.
Enter MARIA

units.speaker: FABIAN
units.text: Here comes my noble gull-catcher.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: Wilt thou set thy foot o' my neck?

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text: Or o' mine either?

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
Shall I play my freedom at tray-trip, and become thy
bondslave?

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text: I' faith, or I either?

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
Why, thou hast put him in such a dream that when the
image of it leaves him he must run mad.

units.speaker: MARIA
units.text: Nay, but say true, does it work upon him?

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: Like aqua vitae with a midwife.

units.speaker: MARIA
units.text:
If you will then see the fruits of the sport, mark his
first approach before my lady. He will come to her in
yellow stockings, and ’tis a color she abhors, and
cross-gartered, a fashion she detests. And he will smile
upon her, which will now be so unsuitable to her
disposition, being addicted to a melancholy as she i s,
that it cannot but turn him into a notable contempt. If
you will see it, follow me.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
To the gates of Tartar, thou most excellent devil of
wit!

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text: I’ll make one too.
Exeunt
Enter VIOLA, and the FOOL playing with a tabor

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text:
Save thee, friend, and thy music. Dost thou live by thy
tabour?

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text: No, sir, I live by the church.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text: Art thou a churchman?

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
No such matter, sir. I do live by the church; for I do
live at my house, and my house doth stand by the
church.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text:
So thou mayst say the king lies by a beggar if a beggar
dwell near him, or the church stands by thy tabor, if
thy tabor stand by the church.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
You have said, sir. To see this age! A sentence is but
a cheveril glove to a good wit. How quickly the wrong
side may be turned outward!

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text:
Nay, that’s certain. They that dally nicely with words
may quickly make them wanton.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text: I would therefore my sister had no name, sir.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text: Why, man?

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
Why, sir, her name’s a word, and to dally with that
word might make my sister wanton. But, indeed, words are
very rascals since bonds disgraced them.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text: Thy reason, man?

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
Troth, sir, I can yield you none without words, and
words are grown so false, I am loath to prove reason
with them.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text:
I warrant thou art a merry fellow and carest for
nothing.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
Not so, sir, I do care for something. But in my
conscience, sir, I do not care for you. If that be to
care for nothing, sir, I would it would make you
invisible.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text: Art not thou the Lady Olivia’s fool?

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
No, indeed, sir; the Lady Olivia has no folly. She will
keep no fool, sir, till she be married, and fools are
as like husbands as pilchards are to herrings; the
husband’s the bigger: I am indeed not her fool, but her
corrupter of words.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text: I saw thee late at the Count Orsino’s.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
Foolery, sir, does walk about the orb like the sun. It
shines everywhere. I would be sorry, sir, but the fool
should be as oft with your master as with my mistress: I
think I saw your wisdom there.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text:
Nay, an thou pass upon me, I’ll no more with thee.
Hold, there’s expenses for thee.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
Now Jove, in his next commodity of hair, send thee a
beard!

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text:
By my troth, I’ll tell thee, I am almost sick for one,
(aside] though I would not have it grow on my chin. [To
fool] Is thy lady within?

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text: Would not a pair of these have bred, sir?

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text: Yes, being kept together and put to use.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
I would play Lord Pandarus of Phrygia, sir, to bring a
Cressida to this Troilus.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text:
[giving him money] I understand you, sir. 'Tis well
begged.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
The matter, I hope, is not great, sir, begging but a
beggar. Cressida was a beggar. My lady is within, sir. I
will construe to them whence you come. Who you are and
what you would are out of my welkin, I might say
“element,” but the word is overworn.
Exit

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text:
This fellow is wise enough to play the fool,
And to do that well craves a kind of wit.
He must observe their mood on whom he jests,
The quality of persons, and the time,
And, like the haggard, check at every feather
That comes before his eye. This is a practise
As full of labor as a wise man’s art,
For folly that he wisely shows is fit.
But wise men, folly-fall'n, quite taint their wit.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: Save you, gentleman.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text: And you, sir.

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text: Dieu vous garde, monsieur.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text: Et vous aussi. Votre serviteur!

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text: I hope, sir, you are, and I am yours.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
Will you encounter the house? My niece is desirous you
should enter, if your trade be to her.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text:
I am bound to your niece, sir. I mean, she is the list
of my voyage.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: Taste your legs, sir. Put them to motion.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text:
My legs do better understand me, sir, than I understand
what you mean by bidding me taste my legs.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: I mean, to go, sir, to enter.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text:
I will answer you with gait and entrance. But we are
prevented.
Enter OLIVIA and MARIA

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text:
Most excellent accomplished lady, the heavens rain
odors on you!

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text:
( aside ) That youth’s a rare courtier. “Rain odors.”
Well.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text:
My matter hath no voice, lady, but to your own most
pregnant and vouchsafed ear.

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text:
[aside] “Odors,” “pregnant,” and “vouchsafed.” I’ll get
'em all three all ready.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text:
Let the garden door be shut, and leave me to my
hearing.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text: My duty, madam, and most humble service.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: What is your name?

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text: Cesario is your servant’s name, fair princess.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text:
My servant, sir! 'Twas never merry world
Since lowly feigning was call’d compliment.
You’re servant to the Count Orsino, youth.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text:
And he is yours, and his must needs be yours:
Your servant’s servant is your servant, madam.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text:
For him, I think not on him. For his thoughts,
Would they were blanks, rather than fill’d with me.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text:
Madam, I come to whet your gentle thoughts
On his behalf.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text:
O, by your leave, I pray you,
I bade you never speak again of him.
But, would you undertake another suit,
I had rather hear you to solicit that
Than music from the spheres.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text: Dear lady—

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text:
Give me leave, beseech you. I did send,
After the last enchantment you did here,
A ring in chase of you. So did I abuse
Myself, my servant, and, I fear me, you:
Under your hard construction must I sit,
To force that on you, in a shameful cunning
Which you knew none of yours. What might you think?
Have you not set mine honor at the stake,
And baited it with all the unmuzzled thoughts
That tyrannous heart can think? To one of your
receiving
Enough is shown. A cypress, not a bosom,
Hides my heart. So, let me hear you speak.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text: I pity you.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: That’s a degree to love.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text:
No, not a grize. For ’tis a vulgar proof
That very oft we pity enemies.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text:
Why then methinks ’tis time to smile again.
O world, how apt the poor are to be proud!
If one should be a prey, how much the better
To fall before the lion than the wolf!
A cloud strikes.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text:
The clock upbraids me with the waste of time.
Be not afraid, good youth, I will not have you.
And yet when wit and youth is come to harvest,
Your wife is like to reap a proper man.
There lies your way, due west.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text:
Then westward ho!
Grace and good disposition attend your ladyship!
You’ll nothing, madam, to my lord by me?

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: Stay, I prithee, tell me what thou thinkest of me.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text: That you do think you are not what you are.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: If I think so, I think the same of you.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text: Then think you right: I am not what I am.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: I would you were as I would have you be!

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text:
Would it be better, madam, than I am?
I wish it might, for now I am your fool.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text:
(aside] Oh, what a deal of scorn looks beautiful
In the contempt and anger of his lip!
A murderous guilt shows not itself more soon
Than love that would seem hid. Love’s night is noon.
[To VIOLA] Cesario, by the roses of the spring,
By maidhood, honor, truth, and everything,
I love thee so, that, maugre all thy pride,
Nor wit nor reason can my passion hide.
Do not extort thy reasons from this clause,
For that I woo, thou therefore hast no cause,
But rather reason thus with reason fetter.
Love sought is good, but given unsought better.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text:
By innocence I swear, and by my youth
I have one heart, one bosom, and one truth,
And that no woman has, nor never none
Shall mistress be of it, save I alone.
And so adieu, good madam. Nevermore
Will I my master’s tears to you deplore.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text:
Yet come again, for thou perhaps mayst move
That heart, which now abhors, to like his love.
Exeunt
Enter SIR TOBY BELCH, SIR ANDREW, and FABIAN

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text: No, faith, I’ll not stay a jot longer.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: Thy reason, dear venom, give thy reason.

units.speaker: FABIAN
units.text: You must needs yield your reason, Sir Andrew.

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text:
Marry, I saw your niece do more favors to the Count’s
servingman than ever she bestowed upon me. I saw’t i'the
orchard.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: Did she see thee the while, old boy? Tell me that.

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text: As plain as I see you now.

units.speaker: FABIAN
units.text: This was a great argument of love in her toward you.

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text: 'Slight, will you make an ass o' me?

units.speaker: FABIAN
units.text:
I will prove it legitimate, sir, upon the oaths of
judgment and reason.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
And they have been grand-jurymen since before Noah was
a sailor.

units.speaker: FABIAN
units.text:
She did show favor to the youth in your sight only to
exasperate you, to awake your dormouse valor, to put
fire in your heart and brimstone in your liver. You
should then have accosted her, and with some excellent
jests, fire-new from the mint, you should have banged
the youth into dumbness. This was looked for at your
hand, and this was balked. The double gilt of this
opportunity you let time wash off, and you are now
sailed into the north of my lady’s opinion, where you
will hang like an icicle on a Dutchman’s beard, unless
you do redeem it by some laudable attempt either of
valor or policy.

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text:
An ’t be any way, it must be with valor, for policy I
hate. I had as lief be a Brownist as a politician.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
Why, then, build me thy fortunes upon the basis of
valor. Challenge me the count’s youth to fight with him.
Hurt him in eleven places. My niece shall take note of
it, and assure thyself, there is no love-broker in the
world can more prevail in man’s commendation with woman
than report of valor.

units.speaker: FABIAN
units.text: There is no way but this, Sir Andrew.

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text: Will either of you bear me a challenge to him?

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
Go, write it in a martial hand. Be curst and brief. It
is no matter how witty, so it be eloquent and full of
invention. Taunt him with the license of ink. If thou
“thou”-est him some thrice, it shall not be amiss; and
as many lies as will lie in thy sheet of paper, although
the sheet were big enough for the bed of Ware in
England, set 'em down. Go, about it. Let there be gall
enough in thy ink, though thou write with a goose-pen,
no matter. About it.

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text: Where shall I find you?

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: We’ll call thee at the cubiculo. Go.

units.speaker: FABIAN
units.text: This is a dear manikin to you, Sir Toby.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
I have been dear to him, lad, some two thousand strong,
or so.

units.speaker: FABIAN
units.text:
We shall have a rare letter from him: but you’ll not
deliver ’t?

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
Never trust me, then. And by all means stir on the
youth to an answer. I think oxen and wainropes cannot
hale them together. For Andrew, if he were opened and
you find so much blood in his liver as will clog the
foot of a flea, I’ll eat the rest of the anatomy.

units.speaker: FABIAN
units.text:
And his opposite, the youth, bears in his visage no
great presage of cruelty.
Enter MARIA

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: Look where the youngest wren of nine comes.

units.speaker: MARIA
units.text:
If you desire the spleen, and will laugh yourself into
stitches, follow me. Yond gull Malvolio is turned
heathen, a very renegado. For there is no Christian that
means to be saved by believing rightly can ever believe
such impossible passages of grossness. He’s in yellow
stockings.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: And cross-gartered?

units.speaker: MARIA
units.text:
Most villanously, like a pedant that keeps a school i'
the church. I have dogged him, like his murderer. He
does obey every point of the letter that I dropped to
betray him. He does smile his face into more lines than
is in the new map with the augmentation of the Indies.
You have not seen such a thing as ’tis. I can hardly
forbear hurling things at him. I know my lady will
strike him. If she do, he’ll smile and take ’t for a
great favor.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: Come, bring us, bring us where he is.
Exeunt
