Enter SEBASTIAN and ANTONIO

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text:
I would not by my will have troubled you,
But, since you make your pleasure of your pains,
I will no further chide you.

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text:
I could not stay behind you. My desire,
More sharp than filèd steel, did spur me forth.
And not all love to see you, though so much
As might have drawn one to a longer voyage,
But jealousy what might befall your travel,
Being skilless in these parts, which to a stranger,
Unguided and unfriended, often prove
Rough and unhospitable. My willing love,
The rather by these arguments of fear,
Set forth in your pursuit.

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text:
My kind Antonio,
I can no other answer make but thanks,
And thanks, and ever thanks. And oft good turns
Are shuffled off with such uncurrent pay.
But were my worth as is my conscience, firm,
You should find better dealing. What’s to do?
Shall we go see the relics of this town?

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text: Tomorrow, sir. Best first go see your lodging.

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text:
I am not weary, and ’tis long to night:
I pray you, let us satisfy our eyes
With the memorials and the things of fame
That do renown this city.

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text:
Would you’d pardon me;
I do not without danger walk these streets:
Once in a sea-fight 'gainst the Count his galleys
I did some service, of such note indeed,
That were I ta'en here it would scarce be answered.

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text: Belike you slew great number of his people?

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text:
The offence is not of such a bloody nature;
Albeit the quality of the time and quarrel
Might well have given us bloody argument.
It might have since been answered in repaying
What we took from them, which, for traffic’s sake,
Most of our city did. Only myself stood out;
For which, if I be lapsèd in this place,
I shall pay dear.

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text: Do not then walk too open.

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text:
It doth not fit me. Hold, sir, here’s my purse.
[giving him money]
In the south suburbs, at the Elephant,
Is best to lodge. I will bespeak our diet,
Whiles you beguile the time and feed your knowledge
With viewing of the town. There shall you have me.

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text: Why I your purse?

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text:
Haply your eye shall light upon some toy
You have desire to purchase, and your store,
I think, is not for idle markets, sir.

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text:
I’ll be your purse-bearer and leave you
For an hour.

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text: To the Elephant.

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text: I do remember.
Exeunt
Enter OLIVIA and MARIA

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text:
I have sent after him. He says he’ll come.
How shall I feast him? What bestow of him?
For youth is bought more oft than begged or borrow’d.
I speak too loud.
(to MARIA) Where’s Malvolio? He is sad and civil
And suits well for a servant with my fortunes.
Where is Malvolio?

units.speaker: MARIA
units.text:
He’s coming, madam; but in very strange manner. He is
sure possessed, madam.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: Why, what’s the matter? Does he rave?

units.speaker: MARIA
units.text:
No, madam, he does nothing but smile. Your ladyship
were best to have some guard about you if he come, for
sure the man is tainted in ’s wits.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: Go call him hither.
Exit MARIA

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text:
I am as mad as he,
If sad and merry madness equal be.
Enter MARIA, with MALVOLIO

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: How now, Malvolio?

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text: Sweet lady, ho, ho.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: Smilest thou? I sent for thee upon a sad occasion.

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text:
Sad, lady! I could be sad. This does make some
obstruction in the blood, this cross-gartering, but what
of that? If it please the eye of one, it is with me as
the very true sonnet is, “Please one, and please all.”

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: Why, how dost thou, man? What is the matter with thee?

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text:
Not black in my mind, though yellow in my legs. It did
come to his hands, and commands shall be executed. I
think we do know the sweet Roman hand.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: Wilt thou go to bed, Malvolio?

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text: To bed? “Ay, sweetheart, and I’ll come to thee.”

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text:
God comfort thee! Why dost thou smile so, and kiss thy
hand so oft?

units.speaker: MARIA
units.text: How do you, Malvolio?

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text: At your request! Yes, nightingales answer daws!

units.speaker: MARIA
units.text:
Why appear you with this ridiculous boldness before my
lady?

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text: “Be not afraid of greatness.” 'Twas well writ.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: What meanest thou by that, Malvolio?

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text: “Some are born great—”

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: Ha?

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text: “Some achieve greatness—”

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: What sayest thou?

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text: “And some have greatness thrust upon them.”

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: Heaven restore thee!

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text: “Remember who commended thy yellow stockings—”

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: Thy yellow stockings?

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text: “And wished to see thee cross-gartered.”

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: Cross-gartered?

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text: “Go to, thou art made, if thou desirest to be so—”

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: Am I made?

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text: “If not, let me see thee a servant still.”

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: Why, this is very midsummer madness.
Enter SERVANT

units.speaker: SERVANT
units.text:
Madam, the young gentleman of the Count Orsino’s is
returned. I could hardly entreat him back. He attends
your ladyship’s pleasure.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: I’ll come to him.
Exit SERVANT

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text:
Good Maria, let this fellow be looked to. Where’s my
cousin Toby? Let some of my people have a special care
of him. I would not have him miscarry for the half of my
dowry.
Exeunt OLIVIA and MARIA

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text:
Oh, ho! Do you come near me now? No worse man than Sir
Toby to look to me. This concurs directly with the
letter. She sends him on purpose that I may appear
stubborn to him, for she incites me to that in the
letter. “Cast thy humble slough,” says she. “Be opposite
with a kinsman, surly with servants. Let thy tongue
tang with arguments of state. Put thyself into the trick
of singularity,” and consequently sets down the manner
how: as, a sad face, a reverend carriage, a slow tongue,
in the habit of some sir of note, and so forth. I have
limed her, but it is Jove’s doing, and Jove make me
thankful! And when she went away now, “Let this fellow
be looked to.” “Fellow!” Not “Malvolio,” nor after my
degree, but “fellow.” Why, everything adheres together,
that no dram of a scruple, no scruple of a scruple, no
obstacle, no incredulous or unsafe circumstance—what can
be said? Nothing that can be can come between me and
the full prospect of my hopes. Well, Jove, not I, is the
doer of this, and he is to be thanked.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
Which way is he, in the name of sanctity? If all the
devils of hell be drawn in little, and Legion himself
possessed him, yet I’ll speak to him.

units.speaker: FABIAN
units.text:
Here he is, here he is. How is’t with you, sir? How
is’t with you, man?

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text: Go off, I discard you. Let me enjoy my private. Go off.

units.speaker: MARIA
units.text:
[t o SIR TOBY BELCH ] Lo, how hollow the fiend speaks
within him! Did not I tell you? Sir Toby, my lady prays
you to have a care of him.

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text: Aha! Does she so?

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
[t o FABIAN and MARIA ] Go to, go to! Peace, peace. We
must deal gently with him. Let me alone. —How do you,
Malvolio? How is ’t with you? What, man, defy the devil!
Consider, he’s an enemy to mankind.

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text: Do you know what you say?

units.speaker: MARIA
units.text:
[t o SIR TOBY BELCH ] La you, an you speak ill of the
devil, how he takes it at heart! Pray God, he be not
bewitched!

units.speaker: FABIAN
units.text: Carry his water to the wisewoman.

units.speaker: MARIA
units.text:
Marry, and it shall be done tomorrow morning if I
live. My lady would not lose him for more than I’ll say.

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text: How now, mistress?

units.speaker: MARIA
units.text: O Lord!

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
[t o MARIA ] Prithee, hold thy peace. This is not the
way. Do you not see you move him? Let me alone with him.

units.speaker: FABIAN
units.text:
No way but gentleness, gently, gently. The fiend is
rough and will not be roughly used.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
[t o MALVOLIO ] Why, how now, my bawcock! How dost thou,
chuck?

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text: Sir!

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
Ay, Biddy, come with me. —What, man! 'Tis not for
gravity to play at cherry-pit with Satan. Hang him, foul
collier!

units.speaker: MARIA
units.text:
Get him to say his prayers, good Sir Toby, get him to
pray.

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text: My prayers, minx?

units.speaker: MARIA
units.text:
[To SIR TOBY BELCH] No, I warrant you, he will not
hear of godliness.

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text:
Go, hang yourselves all! You are idle, shallow things.
I am not of your element. You shall know more hereafter.
Exit

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: Is ’t possible?

units.speaker: FABIAN
units.text:
If this were played upon a stage now, I could condemn
it as an improbable fiction.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
His very genius hath taken the infection of the device,
man.

units.speaker: MARIA
units.text:
Nay, pursue him now, lest the device take air and
taint.

units.speaker: FABIAN
units.text: Why, we shall make him mad indeed.

units.speaker: MARIA
units.text: The house will be the quieter.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
Come, we’ll have him in a dark room and bound. My niece
is already in the belief that he’s mad. We may carry it
thus, for our pleasure and his penance, till our very
pastime, tired out of breath, prompt us to have mercy on
him, at which time we will bring the device to the bar
and crown thee for a finder of madmen. But see, but see!

units.speaker: FABIAN
units.text: More matter for a May morning.

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text:
[presenting a paper] Here’s the challenge, read it.
Warrant there’s vinegar and pepper in ’t.

units.speaker: FABIAN
units.text: Is ’t so saucy?

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text: Ay, is ’t, I warrant him. Do but read.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
Give me. [reads] “Youth, whatsoever thou art, thou art
but a scurvy fellow.”

units.speaker: FABIAN
units.text: Good, and valiant.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
[reads] “Wonder not, nor admire not in thy mind, why I
do call thee so, for I will show thee no reason for ’t.”

units.speaker: FABIAN
units.text: A good note, that keeps you from the blow of the law.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
(reads) “Thou comest to the lady Olivia, and in my
sight she uses thee kindly. But thou liest in thy
throat. That is not the matter I challenge thee for.”

units.speaker: FABIAN
units.text: Very brief, and to exceeding good sense—less.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
[reads] “I will waylay thee going home, where if it be
thy chance to kill me—”

units.speaker: FABIAN
units.text: Good.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: [reads] “Thou killest me like a rogue and a villain.”

units.speaker: FABIAN
units.text: Still you keep o' the windy side of the law. Good.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
[reads] “Fare thee well, and God have mercy upon one
of our souls. He may have mercy upon mine, but my hope
is better, and so look to thyself. Thy friend, as thou
usest him, and thy sworn enemy,
Andrew Aguecheek”
If this letter move him not, his legs cannot. I’ll give
’t him.

units.speaker: MARIA
units.text:
You may have very fit occasion for ’t. He is now in
some commerce with my lady and will by and by depart.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
Go, Sir Andrew. Scout me for him at the corner the
orchard like a bum-baily. So soon as ever thou seest
him, draw, and as thou drawest, swear horrible, for it
comes to pass oft that a terrible oath, with a
swaggering accent sharply twanged off, gives manhood
more approbation than ever proof itself would have
earned him. Away!

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text: Nay, let me alone for swearing.
Exit

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
Now will not I deliver his letter, for the behavior of
the young gentleman gives him out to be of good capacity
and breeding. His employment between his lord and my
niece confirms no less. Therefore this letter, being so
excellently ignorant, will breed no terror in the youth.
He will find it comes from a clodpole. But, sir, I will
deliver his challenge by word of mouth, set upon
Aguecheek a notable report of valor, and drive the
gentleman (as I know his youth will aptly receive it)
into a most hideous opinion of his rage, skill, fury,
and impetuosity. This will so fright them both that they
will kill one another by the look, like cockatrices.
Enter OLIVIA, with VIOLA

units.speaker: FABIAN
units.text:
Here he comes with your niece. Give them way till he
take leave, and presently after him.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
I will meditate the while upon some horrid message for
a challenge.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text:
I have said too much unto a heart of stone
And laid mine honor too unchary on ’t.
There’s something in me that reproves my fault,
But such a headstrong potent fault it is
That it but mocks reproof.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text:
With the same 'havior that your passion bears
Goes on my master’s grief.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text:
Here, wear this jewel for me. 'Tis my picture.
Refuse it not. It hath no tongue to vex you.
And I beseech you come again tomorrow.
What shall you ask of me that I’ll deny,
That honor, saved, may upon asking give?

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text: Nothing but this: your true love for my master.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text:
How with mine honor may I give him that
Which I have given to you?

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text: I will acquit you.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text:
Well, come again tomorrow. Fare thee well.
A fiend like thee might bear my soul to hell.
Exit
Enter SIR TOBY BELCH and FABIAN

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: Gentleman, God save thee.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text: And you, sir.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
That defense thou hast, betake thee to ’t . Of what
nature the wrongs are thou hast done him, I know not,
but thy intercepter, full of despite, bloody as the
hunter, attends thee at the orchard end. Dismount thy
tuck, be yare in thy preparation, for thy assailant is
quick, skillful and deadly.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text:
You mistake, sir. I am sure no man hath any quarrel to
me. My remembrance is very free and clear from any image
of offense done to any man.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
You’ll find it otherwise, I assure you. Therefore, if
you hold your life at any price, betake you to your
guard, for your opposite hath in him what youth,
strength, skill, and wrath can furnish man withal.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text: I pray you, sir, what is he?

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
He is knight, dubbed with unhatched rapier and on
carpet consideration, but he is a devil in private
brawl. Souls and bodies hath he divorced three, and his
incensement at this moment is so implacable that
satisfaction can be none but by pangs of death and
sepulchre. Hob, nob, is his word. “Give ’t or take ’t.”

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text:
I will return again into the house and desire some
conduct of the lady. I am no fighter. I have heard of
some kind of men that put quarrels purposely on others,
to taste their valor. Belike this is a man of that
quirk.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
Sir, no. His indignation derives itself out of a very
competent injury. Therefore get you on and give him his
desire. Back you shall not to the house, unless you
undertake that with me which with as much safety you
might answer him. Therefore on, or strip your sword
stark naked, for meddle you must, that’s certain, or
forswear to wear iron about you.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text:
This is as uncivil as strange. I beseech you, do me
this courteous office, as to know of the knight what my
offense to him is. It is something of my negligence,
nothing of my purpose.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
I will do so. Signior Fabian, stay you by this
gentleman till my return.
Exit

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text: Pray you, sir, do you know of this matter?

units.speaker: FABIAN
units.text:
I know the knight is incensed against you, even to a
mortal arbitrament, but nothing of the circumstance
more.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text: I beseech you, what manner of man is he?

units.speaker: FABIAN
units.text:
Nothing of that wonderful promise, to read him by his
form, as you are like to find him in the proof of his
valor. He is, indeed, sir, the most skillful, bloody,
and fatal opposite that you could possibly have found in
any part of Illyria. Will you walk towards him? I will
make your peace with him if I can.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text:
I shall be much bound to you for ’t. I am one that had
rather go with sir priest than sir knight. I care not
who knows so much of my mettle.
Exeunt
Enter SIR TOBY BELCH, with SIR ANDREW

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
Why, man, he’s a very devil. I have not seen such a
firago. I had a pass with him, rapier, scabbard, and
all, and he gives me the stuck-in with such a mortal
motion, that it is inevitable. And on the answer, he
pays you as surely as your feet hit the ground they step
on. They say he has been fencer to the Sophy.

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text: Pox on ’t! I’ll not meddle with him.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
Ay, but he will not now be pacified. Fabian can scarce
hold him yonder.

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text:
Plague on ’t, an I thought he had been valiant and so
cunning in fence, I’d have seen him damned ere I’d have
challenged him. Let him let the matter slip, and I’ll
give him my horse, gray Capilet.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
I’ll make the motion. Stand here, make a good show on
’t.
This shall end without the perdition of souls. [aside]
Marry,
I’ll ride your horse as well as I ride you.
Enter FABIAN and VIOLA

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
[To FABIAN ] I have his horse to take up the quarrel. I
have persuaded him the youth’s a devil.

units.speaker: FABIAN
units.text:
He is as horribly conceited of him, and pants and looks
pale, as if a bear were at his heels.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
(t o VIOLA ) There’s no remedy, sir; he will fight with
you for ’s oath sake. Marry, he hath better bethought
him of his quarrel, and he finds that now scarce to be
worth talking of. Therefore, draw for the supportance of
his vow. He protests he will not hurt you.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text:
[aside] Pray God defend me! A little thing would make
me tell them how much I lack of a man.

units.speaker: FABIAN
units.text: Give ground, if you see him furious.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
Come, Sir Andrew, there’s no remedy. The gentleman
will, for his honor’s sake, have one bout with you. He
cannot by the duello avoid it. But he has promised me,
as he is a gentleman and a soldier, he will not hurt
you. Come on, to ’t.

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text: Pray God, he keep his oath!

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text: I do assure you, ’tis against my will.
They draw swords Enter ANTONIO

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text:
Put up your sword. If this young gentleman
Have done offence, I take the fault on me.
If you offend him, I for him defy you.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: You, sir? Why, what are you?

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text:
One, sir, that for his love dares yet do more
Than you have heard him brag to you he will.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: Nay, if you be an undertaker, I am for you.
They draw swords Enter OFFICERS

units.speaker: FABIAN
units.text: O good Sir Toby, hold! Here come the officers.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: [t o ANTONIO ] I’ll be with you anon.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text:
[t o ANDREW ] Pray, sir, put your sword up, if you
please.

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text:
Marry, will I, sir. And for that I promised you, I’ll
be as good as my word. He will bear you easily and reins
well.

units.speaker: FIRST OFFICER
units.text: This is the man. Do thy office.

units.speaker: SECOND OFFICER
units.text: Antonio, I arrest thee at the suit of Count Orsino.

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text: You do mistake me, sir.

units.speaker: FIRST OFFICER
units.text:
No, sir, no jot. I know your favor well,
Though now you have no sea-cap on your head.—
Take him away. He knows I know him well.

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text:
I must obey. (t o VIOLA ) This comes with seeking you:
But there’s no remedy. I shall answer it .
What will you do, now my necessity
Makes me to ask you for my purse? It grieves me
Much more for what I cannot do for you
Than what befalls myself. You stand amazed,
But be of comfort.

units.speaker: SECOND OFFICER
units.text: Come, sir, away.

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text: [t o VIOLA ] I must entreat of you some of that money.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text:
What money, sir?
For the fair kindness you have showed me here,
And part being prompted by your present trouble,
Out of my lean and low ability
I’ll lend you something. My having is not much.
I’ll make division of my present with you.
Hold, there’s half my coffer. (offering him money)

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text:
Will you deny me now?
Is ’t possible that my deserts to you
Can lack persuasion? Do not tempt my misery,
Lest that it make me so unsound a man
As to upbraid you with those kindnesses
That I have done for you.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text:
I know of none,
Nor know I you by voice or any feature.
I hate ingratitude more in a man
Than lying, vainness, babbling, drunkenness,
Or any taint of vice whose strong corruption
Inhabits our frail blood—

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text: O heavens themselves!

units.speaker: SECOND OFFICER
units.text: Come, sir, I pray you, go.

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text:
Let me speak a little. This youth that you see here
I snatched one half out of the jaws of death,
Relieved him with such sanctity of love,
And to his image, which methought did promise
Most venerable worth, did I devotion.

units.speaker: FIRST OFFICER
units.text: What’s that to us? The time goes by. Away!

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text:
But oh, how vile an idol proves this god!
Thou hast, Sebastian, done good feature shame.
In nature there’s no blemish but the mind.
None can be called deformed but the unkind.
Virtue is beauty, but the beauteous evil
Are empty trunks o'erflourished by the devil.

units.speaker: FIRST OFFICER
units.text: The man grows mad. Away with him. Come, come, sir.

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text: Lead me on.
Exit with OFFICERS

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text:
Methinks his words do from such passion fly,
That he believes himself. So do not I.
Prove true, imagination, oh, prove true,
That I, dear brother, be now ta'en for you!

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
Come hither, knight. Come hither, Fabian. We’ll whisper
o'er a couplet or two of most sage saws.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text:
He named Sebastian. I my brother know
Yet living in my glass. Even such and so
In favor was my brother, and he went
Still in this fashion, color, ornament,
For him I imitate. Oh, if it prove,
Tempests are kind and salt waves fresh in love!
Exit

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
A very dishonest paltry boy, and more a coward than a
hare. His dishonesty appears in leaving his friend here
in necessity and denying him. And for his cowardship,
ask Fabian.

units.speaker: FABIAN
units.text: A coward, a most devout coward, religious in it.

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text: 'Slid, I’ll after him again and beat him.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: Do, cuff him soundly, but never draw thy sword.

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text: An I do not—

units.speaker: FABIAN
units.text: Come, let’s see the event.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: I dare lay any money ’twill be nothing yet.
Exeunt
Enter SEBASTIAN and FOOL

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text: Will you make me believe that I am not sent for you?

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text:
Go to, go to, thou art a foolish fellow. Let me be
clear of thee.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
Well held out, i' faith. No, I do not know you, nor I
am not sent to you by my lady, to bid you come speak
with her, nor your name is not Master Cesario, nor this
is not my nose neither. Nothing that is so is so.

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text:
I prithee, vent thy folly somewhere else. Thou know’st
not me.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
Vent my folly? He has heard that word of some great
man and now applies it to a fool. Vent my folly! I am
afraid this great lubber, the world, will prove a
cockney. I prithee now, ungird thy strangeness and tell
me what I shall vent to my lady. Shall I vent to her
that thou art coming?

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text:
I prithee, foolish Greek, depart from me. There’s
money for thee. [giving money] If you tarry longer, I
shall give worse payment.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
By my troth, thou hast an open hand. These wise men
that give fools money get themselves a good report—after
fourteen years' purchase.

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text:
[To SEBASTIAN] Now, sir, have I met you again? There’s
for you.

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text:
[returning the blow] Why, there’s for thee, and there,
and there. Are all the people mad?

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: Hold, sir, or I’ll throw your dagger o'er the house.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
[aside] This will I tell my lady straight. I would not
be in some of your coats for two pence.
Exit

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: ( seizi ng SEBASTIAN) Come on, sir, hold!

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text:
Nay, let him alone. I’ll go another way to work with
him. I’ll have an action of battery against him if there
be any law in Illyria. Though I struck him first, yet
it’s no matter for that.

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text: [To SIR TOBY BELCH] Let go thy hand.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
Come, sir, I will not let you go. Come, my young
soldier, put up your iron. You are well fleshed. Come
on.

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text: I will be free from thee.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
What, what? Nay, then I must have an ounce or two of
this malapert blood from you.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: Hold, Toby! On thy life I charge thee, hold!

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: Madam!

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text:
Will it be ever thus? Ungracious wretch,
Fit for the mountains and the barbarous caves,
Where manners ne'er were preach’d! Out of my sight!—
Be not offended, dear Cesario.—
Rudesby, be gone!

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text:
( aside ) What relish is in this? How runs the stream?
Or I am mad, or else this is a dream.
Let fancy still my sense in Lethe steep.
If it be thus to dream, still let me sleep!

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: Nay, come, I prithee. Would thou’dst be ruled by me!

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text: Madam, I will.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: Oh, say so, and so be!
Exeunt
Enter MARIA and FOOL

units.speaker: MARIA
units.text:
Nay, I prithee, put on this gown and this beard. Make
him believe thou art Sir Topas the curate. Do it
quickly. I’ll call Sir Toby the whilst.
Exit

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
Well, I’ll put it on, and I will dissemble myself in
’t, and I would I were the first that ever dissembled in
such a gown.
FOOL puts on gown and beard

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
I am not tall enough to become the function well, nor
lean enough to be thought a good student, but to be said
an honest man and a good housekeeper goes as fairly as
to say a careful man and a great scholar. The
competitors enter.
Enter SIR TOBY BELCH and MARIA

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: Jove bless thee, master Parson.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
Bonos dies, Sir Toby. For, as the old hermit of Prague,
that never saw pen and ink, very wittily said to a
niece of King Gorboduc, “That that is is.” So I, being
Master Parson, am Master Parson. For, what is “that” but
“that,” and “is” but “is”?

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: To him, Sir Topas.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
( disguising his voice ) What ho, I say! Peace in this
prison!

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: The knave counterfeits well. A good knave.

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text: ( from within ) Who calls there?

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
Sir Topas the curate, who comes to visit Malvolio the
lunatic.

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text: Sir Topas, Sir Topas, good Sir Topas, go to my lady—

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
Out, hyperbolical fiend! How vexest thou this man!
Talkest thou nothing but of ladies?

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: ( aside ) Well said, Master Parson.

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text:
Sir Topas, never was man thus wronged. Good Sir Topas,
do not think I am mad. They have laid me here in
hideous darkness.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
Fie, thou dishonest Satan! I call thee by the most
modest terms, for I am one of those gentle ones that
will use the devil himself with courtesy. Sayest thou
that house is dark?

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text: As hell, Sir Topas.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
Why, it hath bay windows transparent as barricadoes,
and the clerestories toward the south-north are as
lustrous as ebony. And yet complainest thou of
obstruction?

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text:
I am not mad, Sir Topas. I say to you this house is
dark.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
Madman, thou errest. I say, there is no darkness but
ignorance, in which thou art more puzzled than the
Egyptians in their fog.

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text:
I say, this house is as dark as ignorance, though
ignorance were as dark as hell. And I say, there was
never man thus abused. I am no more mad than you are.
Make the trial of it in any constant question.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text: What is the opinion of Pythagoras concerning wildfowl?

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text:
That the soul of our grandam might haply inhabit a
bird.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text: What thinkest thou of his opinion?

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text:
I think nobly of the soul, and no way approve his
opinion.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
Fare thee well. Remain thou still in darkness. Thou
shalt hold the opinion of Pythagoras ere I will allow of
thy wits, and fear to kill a woodcock lest thou
dispossess the soul of thy grandam. Fare thee well.

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text: Sir Topas, Sir Topas!

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text: My most exquisite Sir Topas!

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text: Nay, I am for all waters.

units.speaker: MARIA
units.text:
Thou mightst have done this without thy beard and gown.
He sees thee not.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
To him in thine own voice, and bring me word how thou
findest him. I would we were well rid of this knavery.
If he may be conveniently delivered, I would he were,
for I am now so far in offense with my niece that I
cannot pursue with any safety this sport to the upshot.
Come by and by to my chamber.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
[sings in his own voice]
Hey, Robin, jolly Robin,
Tell me how thy lady does.

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text: Fool!

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text: (sings) My lady is unkind, perdy.

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text: Fool!

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text: (sings) Alas, why is she so?

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text: Fool, I say!

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text: (sings) She loves another—Who calls, ha?

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text:
Good fool, as ever thou wilt deserve well at my hand,
help me to a candle, and pen, ink, and paper. As I am a
gentleman, I will live to be thankful to thee for ’t.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text: Master Malvolio?

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text: Ay, good fool.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text: Alas, sir, how fell you besides your five wits?

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text:
Fool, there was never a man so notoriously abused: I
am as well in my wits, Fool, as thou art.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
But as well? Then you are mad indeed, if you be no
better in your wits than a fool.

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text:
They have here propertied me, keep me in darkness, send
ministers to me —asses!—and do all they can to face me
out of my wits.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
Advise you what you say. The minister is here. [in the
voice of Sir Topas] Malvolio, Malvolio, thy wits the
heavens restore! Endeavor thyself to sleep, and leave
thy vain bibble-babble.

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text: Sir Topas!

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
[as Sir Topas] Maintain no words with him, good fellow.
[in his own voice] Who, I, sir? Not I, sir. God b' wi'
you, good Sir Topas. [as Sir Topas] Marry, amen. [in his
own voice] I will, sir, I will.

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text: Fool, fool, fool, I say!

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
Alas, sir, be patient. What say you sir? I am shent for
speaking to you.

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text:
Good fool, help me to some light and some paper. I tell
thee, I am as well in my wits as any man in Illyria.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text: Well-a-day that you were, sir.

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text:
By this hand, I am. Good fool, some ink, paper, and
light, and convey what I will set down to my lady. It
shall advantage thee more than ever the bearing of
letter did.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
I will help you to ’t. But tell me true, are you not
mad indeed? Or do you but counterfeit?

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text: Believe me, I am not. I tell thee true.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
Nay, I’ll ne'er believe a madman till I see his brains.
I will fetch you light, and paper, and ink.

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text:
Fool, I’ll requite it in the highest degree. I prithee,
be gone.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
[sings]
I am gone, sir,
And anon, sir,
I’ll be with you again,
In a trice,
Like to the old Vice,
Your need to sustain,
Who, with dagger of lath
In his rage and his wrath,
Cries “Aha,” to the devil,
Like a mad lad,
“Pare thy nails, dad,
Adieu, goodman devil.”
Exit
Enter SEBASTIAN

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text:
This is the air, that is the glorious sun.
This pearl she gave me, I do feel ’t and see ’t,
And though ’tis wonder that enwraps me thus,
Yet ’tis not madness. Where’s Antonio, then?
I could not find him at the Elephant.
Yet there he was, and there I found this credit,
That he did range the town to seek me out.
His counsel now might do me golden service.
For though my soul disputes well with my sense
That this may be some error, but no madness,
Yet doth this accident and flood of fortune
So far exceed all instance, all discourse,
That I am ready to distrust mine eyes
And wrangle with my reason that persuades me
To any other trust but that I am mad—
Or else the lady’s mad. Yet if ’twere so,
She could not sway her house, command her followers,
Take and give back affairs and their dispatch
With such a smooth, discreet, and stable bearing
As I perceive she does. There’s something in ’t
That is deceivable. But here the lady comes.
Enter OLIVIA and PRIEST

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text:
[t o SEBASTIAN ]
Blame not this haste of mine. If you mean well,
Now go with me and with this holy man
Into the chantry by. There, before him
And underneath that consecrated roof,
Plight me the full assurance of your faith,
That my most jealous and too doubtful soul
May live at peace. He shall conceal it
Whiles you are willing it shall come to note,
What time we will our celebration keep
According to my birth. What do you say?

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text:
I’ll follow this good man, and go with you;
And, having sworn truth, ever will be true.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text:
Then lead the way, good father; and heavens so shine
That they may fairly note this act of mine.
Exeunt
Enter FOOL and FABIAN

units.speaker: FABIAN
units.text: Now, as thou lovest me, let me see his letter.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text: Good Master Fabian, grant me another request.

units.speaker: FABIAN
units.text: Anything.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text: Do not desire to see this letter.

units.speaker: FABIAN
units.text:
This is, to give a dog and in recompense desire my dog
again.
Enter ORSINO, VIOLA, CURIO, and lords

units.speaker: ORSINO
units.text: Belong you to the Lady Olivia, friends?

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text: Ay, sir, we are some of her trappings.

units.speaker: ORSINO
units.text: I know thee well. How dost thou, my good fellow?

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
Truly, sir, the better for my foes and the worse for
my friends.

units.speaker: ORSINO
units.text: Just the contrary. The better for thy friends.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text: No, sir, the worse.

units.speaker: ORSINO
units.text: How can that be?

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
Marry, sir, they praise me and make an ass of me, now
my foes tell me plainly I am an ass. So that by my foes,
sir I profit in the knowledge of myself, and by my
friends, I am abused. So that, conclusions to be as
kisses, if your four negatives make your two
affirmatives, why then the worse for my friends and the
better for my foes.

units.speaker: ORSINO
units.text: Why, this is excellent.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
By my troth, sir, no—though it please you to be one of
my friends.

units.speaker: ORSINO
units.text:
[giving a coin]
Thou shalt not be the worse for me: there’s gold.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
But that it would be double-dealing, sir, I would you
could make it another.

units.speaker: ORSINO
units.text: O, you give me ill counsel.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
Put your grace in your pocket, sir, for this once, and
let your flesh and blood obey it.

units.speaker: ORSINO
units.text:
Well, I will be so much a sinner, to be a
double-dealer.
There’s another. ( giving a coin )

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
Primo, secundo, tertio is a good play, and the old
saying is, the third pays for all. The triplex, sir, is
a good tripping measure, or the bells of Saint Bennet,
sir, may put you in mind—one, two, three.

units.speaker: ORSINO
units.text:
You can fool no more money out of me at this throw. If
you will let your lady know I am here to speak with her,
and bring her along with you, it may awake my bounty
further.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
Marry, sir, lullaby to your bounty till I come again.
I go, sir, but I would not have you to think that my
desire of having is the sin of covetousness. But, as you
say, sir, let your bounty take a nap, I will awake it
anon.
Exit

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text: Here comes the man, sir, that did rescue me.
Enter ANTONIO and OFFICERS

units.speaker: ORSINO
units.text:
That face of his I do remember well.
Yet, when I saw it last, it was besmeared
As black as Vulcan in the smoke of war.
A baubling vessel was he captain of,
For shallow draught and bulk unprizable,
With which such scathful grapple did he make
With the most noble bottom of our fleet,
That very envy and the tongue of loss
Cried fame and honor on him. —What’s the matter?

units.speaker: FIRST OFFICER
units.text:
Orsino, this is that Antonio
That took the Phoenix and her fraught from Candy,
And this is he that did the Tiger board
When your young nephew Titus lost his leg.
Here in the streets, desperate of shame and state,
In private brabble did we apprehend him.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text:
He did me kindness, sir, drew on my side,
But in conclusion put strange speech upon me.
I know not what ’twas but distraction.

units.speaker: ORSINO
units.text:
Notable pirate! Thou saltwater thief,
What foolish boldness brought thee to their mercies,
Whom thou, in terms so bloody and so dear,
Hast made thine enemies?

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text:
Orsino, noble sir,
Be pleased that I shake off these names you give me.
Antonio never yet was thief or pirate,
Though, I confess, on base and ground enough,
Orsino’s enemy. A witchcraft drew me hither.
That most ingrateful boy there by your side
From the rude sea’s enraged and foamy mouth
Did I redeem. A wreck past hope he was.
His life I gave him and did thereto add
My love, without retention or restraint,
All his in dedication. For his sake
Did I expose myself, pure for his love,
Into the danger of this adverse town,
Drew to defend him when he was beset,
Where being apprehended, his false cunning,
(Not meaning to partake with me in danger)
Taught him to face me out of his acquaintance,
And grew a twenty-years-removed thing
While one would wink, denied me mine own purse,
Which I had recommended to his use
Not half an hour before.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text: How can this be?

units.speaker: ORSINO
units.text: [To ANTONIO] When came he to this town?

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text:
Today, my lord, and for three months before,
No interim, not a minute’s vacancy,
Both day and night did we keep company.
Enter OLIVIA and attendants

units.speaker: ORSINO
units.text:
Here comes the Countess. Now heaven walks on earth.
But for thee, fellow. Fellow, thy words are madness:
Three months this youth hath tended upon me;
But more of that anon. [To an officer] Take him
aside.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text:
What would my lord, but that he may not have,
Wherein Olivia may seem serviceable?
Cesario, you do not keep promise with me.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text: Madam?

units.speaker: ORSINO
units.text: Gracious Olivia—

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: What do you say, Cesario?—Good my lord—

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text: My lord would speak. My duty hushes me.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text:
If it be aught to the old tune, my lord,
It is as fat and fulsome to mine ear
As howling after music.

units.speaker: ORSINO
units.text: Still so cruel?

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: Still so constant, lord.

units.speaker: ORSINO
units.text:
What, to perverseness? You, uncivil lady,
To whose ingrate and unauspicious altars
My soul the faithfull’st off'rings have breathed out
That e'er devotion tendered —what shall I do?

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: Even what it please my lord that shall become him.

units.speaker: ORSINO
units.text:
Why should I not, had I the heart to do it,
Like to the Egyptian thief at point of death,
Kill what I love? —A savage jealousy
That sometimes savors nobly. But hear me this:
Since you to nonregardance cast my faith,
And that I partly know the instrument
That screws me from my true place in your favor,
Live you the marble-breasted tyrant still.
But this your minion, whom I know you love,
And whom, by heaven I swear, I tender dearly,
Him will I tear out of that cruel eye
Where he sits crowned in his master’s spite.
[To VIOLA] Come, boy, with me. My thoughts are ripe in
mischief:
I’ll sacrifice the lamb that I do love
To spite a raven’s heart within a dove.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text:
And I, most jocund, apt, and willingly,
To do you rest, a thousand deaths would die.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: Where goes Cesario?

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text:
After him I love
More than I love these eyes, more than my life,
More, by all mores, than e'er I shall love wife.
If I do feign, you witnesses above,
Punish my life for tainting of my love!

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: Ay me, detested! How am I beguiled!

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text: Who does beguile you? Who does do you wrong?

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text:
Hast thou forgot thyself? Is it so long?—
Call forth the holy father.
Exit an attendant

units.speaker: ORSINO
units.text:
[To VIOLA]
Come, away!

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: Whither, my lord?—Cesario, husband, stay.

units.speaker: ORSINO
units.text: Husband?

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: Ay, husband. Can he that deny?

units.speaker: ORSINO
units.text: Her husband, sirrah?

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text: No, my lord, not I.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text:
Alas, it is the baseness of thy fear
That makes thee strangle thy propriety.
Fear not, Cesario. Take thy fortunes up.
Be that thou know’st thou art, and then thou art
As great as that thou fear’st.
Enter PRIEST

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text:
O, welcome, father!
Father, I charge thee, by thy reverence,
Here to unfold (though lately we intended
To keep in darkness what occasion now
Reveals before ’tis ripe) what thou dost know
Hath newly passed between this youth and me.

units.speaker: PRIEST
units.text:
A contract of eternal bond of love,
Confirmed by mutual joinder of your hands,
Attested by the holy close of lips,
Strengthened by interchangement of your rings,
And all the ceremony of this compact
Sealed in my function, by my testimony,
Since when, my watch hath told me, toward my grave
I have traveled but two hours.

units.speaker: ORSINO
units.text:
O thou dissembling cub! What wilt thou be
When time hath sowed a grizzle on thy case?
Or will not else thy craft so quickly grow
That thine own trip shall be thine overthrow?
Farewell, and take her; but direct thy feet
Where thou and I henceforth may never meet.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text: My lord, I do protest—

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text:
O, do not swear!
Hold little faith, though thou hast too much fear.

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text:
For the love of God, a surgeon! Send one presently to
Sir
Toby.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: What’s the matter?

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text:
He has broke my head across and has given Sir Toby a
bloody coxcomb too. For the love of God, your help! I
had rather than forty pound I were at home.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: Who has done this, Sir Andrew?

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text:
The Count’s gentleman, one Cesario. We took him for a
coward, but he’s the very devil incardinate.

units.speaker: ORSINO
units.text: My gentleman, Cesario?

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text:
'Od’s lifelings, here he is!—You broke my head for
nothing, and that that I did, I was set on to do ’t by
Sir Toby.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text:
Why do you speak to me? I never hurt you.
You drew your sword upon me without cause,
But I bespoke you fair and hurt you not.

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text:
If a bloody coxcomb be a hurt, you have hurt me. I
think you set nothing by a bloody coxcomb.

units.speaker: ORSINO
units.text: How now, gentleman? How is ’t with you?

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
That’s all one: has hurt me, and there’s the end on ’t.
[To FOOL] Sot, didst see Dick Surgeon, sot?

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
Oh, he’s drunk, Sir Toby, an hour agone. His eyes were
set at eight i' the morning.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
Then he’s a rogue, and a passy-measures pavin. I hate a
drunken rogue.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: Away with him! Who hath made this havoc with them?

units.speaker: SIR ANDREW
units.text:
I’ll help you, Sir Toby, because we’ll be dressed
together.

units.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
units.text:
Will you help?—An ass-head, and a coxcomb, and a knave,
a thin-faced knave, a gull!

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: Get him to bed, and let his hurt be looked to.
Enter SEBASTIAN

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text:
I am sorry, madam, I have hurt your kinsman,
But, had it been the brother of my blood,
I must have done no less with wit and safety.
You throw a strange regard upon me, and by that
I do perceive it hath offended you.
Pardon me, sweet one, even for the vows
We made each other but so late ago.

units.speaker: ORSINO
units.text:
One face, one voice, one habit, and two persons!
A natural perspective, that is and is not!

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text:
Antonio, O my dear Antonio!
How have the hours racked and tortured me
Since I have lost thee!

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text: Sebastian are you?

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text: Fear’st thou that, Antonio?

units.speaker: ANTONIO
units.text:
How have you made division of yourself?
An apple, cleft in two, is not more twin
Than these two creatures. Which is Sebastian?

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: Most wonderful!

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text:
[looking at VIOLA] Do I stand there? I never had a
brother;
Nor can there be that deity in my nature,
Of here and everywhere. I had a sister,
Whom the blind waves and surges have devoured.
Of charity, what kin are you to me?
What countryman? What name? What parentage?

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text:
Of Messaline. Sebastian was my father;
Such a Sebastian was my brother too,
So went he suited to his watery tomb .
If spirits can assume both form and suit
You come to fright us.

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text:
A spirit I am indeed,
But am in that dimension grossly clad
Which from the womb I did participate.
Were you a woman, as the rest goes even,
I should my tears let fall upon your cheek
And say “Thrice-welcome, drownèd Viola!”

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text: My father had a mole upon his brow.

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text: And so had mine.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text:
And died that day when Viola from her birth
Had numbered thirteen years.

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text:
Oh, that record is lively in my soul!
He finished indeed his mortal act
That day that made my sister thirteen years.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text:
If nothing lets to make us happy both
But this my masculine usurped attire,
Do not embrace me till each circumstance
Of place, time, fortune, do cohere and jump
That I am Viola. Which to confirm,
I’ll bring you to a captain in this town,
Where lie my maiden weeds, by whose gentle help
I was preserved to serve this noble count.
All the occurrence of my fortune since
Hath been between this lady and this lord.

units.speaker: SEBASTIAN
units.text:
[To OLIVIA] So comes it, lady, you have been mistook.
But nature to her bias drew in that.
You would have been contracted to a maid;
Nor are you therein, by my life, deceived.
You are betrothed both to a maid and man.

units.speaker: ORSINO
units.text:
[To OLIVIA] Be not amazed. Right noble is his blood.
If this be so, as yet the glass seems true,
I shall have share in this most happy wreck.
[To VIOLA] Boy, thou hast said to me a thousand times
Thou never shouldst love woman like to me.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text:
And all those sayings will I overswear;
And those swearings keep as true in soul
As doth that orbèd continent the fire
That severs day from night.

units.speaker: ORSINO
units.text:
Give me thy hand,
And let me see thee in thy woman’s weeds.

units.speaker: VIOLA
units.text:
The captain that did bring me first on shore
Hath my maid’s garments. He, upon some action,
Is now in durance at Malvolio’s suit,
A gentleman and follower of my lady’s.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: He shall enlarge him.
Enter FOOL with a letter, and FABIAN

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text:
Fetch Malvolio hither:
And yet, alas, now I remember me,
They say, poor gentleman, he’s much distract.
A most extracting frenzy of mine own
From my remembrance clearly banished his.
(to FOOL) How does he, sirrah?

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
Truly, madam, he holds Beelzebub at the staves' end as
well as a man in his case may do. Has here writ a letter
to you. I should have given ’t you today morning, but
as a madman’s epistles are no gospels, so it skills not
much when they are delivered.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: Open ’t, and read it.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
Look then to be well edified when the fool delivers the
madman. [reads] “By the Lord, madam,”—

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: How now? Art thou mad?

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
No, madam, I do but read madness. An your ladyship will
have it as it ought to be, you must allow vox.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: Prithee, read i' thy right wits.

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
So I do, madonna. But to read his right wits is to read
thus.
Therefore perpend, my princess, and give ear.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: [giving the letter to FABIAN] Read it you, sirrah.

units.speaker: FABIAN
units.text:
[reads]
“By the Lord, madam, you wrong me, and the world shall
know it. Though you have put me into darkness and given
your drunken cousin rule over me, yet have I the benefit
of my senses as well as your Ladyship. I have your own
letter that induced me to the semblance I put on, with
the which I doubt not but to do myself much right or you
much shame. Think of me as you please. I leave my duty
a little unthought of and speak out of my injury. The
madly used Malvolio.”

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: Did he write this?

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text: Ay, madam.

units.speaker: ORSINO
units.text: This savors not much of distraction.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: See him delivered, Fabian; bring him hither.
Exit FABIAN

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text:
My lord so please you, these things further thought
on,
To think me as well a sister as a wife,
One day shall crown the alliance on ’t, so please you,
Here at my house and at my proper cost.

units.speaker: ORSINO
units.text:
Madam, I am most apt to embrace your offer.
(to VIOLA) Your master quits you, and for your service
done him,
So much against the mettle of your sex,
So far beneath your soft and tender breeding,
And since you called me “master” for so long,
Here is my hand. You shall from this time be
Your master’s mistress.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: [To VIOLA] A sister! You are she.
Enter FABIAN, with MALVOLIO

units.speaker: ORSINO
units.text: Is this the madman?

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text:
Ay, my lord, this same.
How now, Malvolio!

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text:
Madam, you have done me wrong,
Notorious wrong.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: Have I, Malvolio? No.

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text:
(handing a paper)
Lady, you have. Pray you, peruse that letter.
You must not now deny it is your hand.
Write from it if you can, in hand or phrase;
Or say ’tis not your seal, not your invention:
You can say none of this. Well, grant it then
And tell me, in the modesty of honor,
Why you have given me such clear lights of favor,
Bade me come smiling and cross-gartered to you,
To put on yellow stockings, and to frown
Upon Sir Toby and the lighter people?
And, acting this in an obedient hope,
Why have you suffered me to be imprisoned,
Kept in a dark house, visited by the priest,
And made the most notorious geck and gull
That e'er invention played on? Tell me why.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text:
Alas, Malvolio, this is not my writing,
Though, I confess, much like the character.
But out of question, ’tis Maria’s hand.
And now I do bethink me, it was she
First told me thou wast mad, then camest in smiling,
And in such forms which here were presupposed
Upon thee in the letter. Prithee, be content.
This practice hath most shrewdly passed upon thee;
But when we know the grounds and authors of it,
Thou shalt be both the plaintiff and the judge
Of thine own cause.

units.speaker: FABIAN
units.text:
Good madam, hear me speak,
And let no quarrel nor no brawl to come
Taint the condition of this present hour,
Which I have wonder’d at. In hope it shall not,
Most freely I confess, myself and Toby
Set this device against Malvolio here,
Upon some stubborn and uncourteous parts
We had conceived against him. Maria writ
The letter at Sir Toby’s great importance,
In recompense whereof he hath married her.
How with a sportful malice it was followed,
May rather pluck on laughter than revenge,
If that the injuries be justly weighed
That have on both sides passed.

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text:
[To MALVOLIO] Alas, poor fool, how have they baffled
thee!

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
Why, “some are born great, some achieve greatness, and
some have greatness thrown upon them.” I was one, sir,
in this interlude, one Sir Topas, sir, but that’s all
one. [imitates MALVOLIO] “By the Lord, fool, I am not
mad.” —But do you remember? “Madam, why laugh you at such
a barren rascal; an you smile not, he’s gagged?” and
thus the whirligig of time brings in his revenges.

units.speaker: MALVOLIO
units.text: I’ll be revenged on the whole pack of you.
Exit

units.speaker: OLIVIA
units.text: He hath been most notoriously abused.

units.speaker: ORSINO
units.text: Pursue him and entreat him to a peace.
Some exit

units.speaker: ORSINO
units.text:
He hath not told us of the captain yet.
When that is known and golden time convents,
A solemn combination shall be made
Of our dear souls.— Meantime, sweet sister,
We will not part from hence. Cesario, come,
For so you shall be, while you are a man.
But when in other habits you are seen,
Orsino’s mistress and his fancy’s queen.
Exeunt all, except FOOL

units.speaker: FOOL
units.text:
[sings]
When that I was and a little tiny boy,
With hey, ho, the wind and the rain,
A foolish thing was but a toy,
For the rain it raineth every day.
But when I came to man’s estate,
With hey, ho, the wind and the rain,
'Gainst knaves and thieves men shut their gate,
For the rain it raineth every day.
But when I came, alas! to wive,
With hey, ho, the wind and the rain,
By swaggering could I never thrive,
For the rain it raineth every day.
But when I came unto my beds,
With hey, ho, the wind and the rain,
With toss-pots still had drunken heads,
For the rain it raineth every day.
A great while ago the world begun,
With hey, ho, the wind and the rain,
But that’s all one, our play is done,
And we’ll strive to please you every day.
Exit

sentences.text: Enter ORSINO, CURIO, and other lords; Musicians playing

sentences.speaker: ORSINO
sentences.text:
If music be the food of love, play on.
Give me excess of it that, surfeiting,
The appetite may sicken, and so die.
That strain again, it had a dying fall.
O, it came o'er my ear like the sweet sound,
That breathes upon a bank of violets,
Stealing and giving odor. Enough, no more.
'Tis not so sweet now as it was before.
O spirit of love, how quick and fresh art thou,
That, notwithstanding thy capacity
Receiveth as the sea, nought enters there,
Of what validity and pitch soe'er,
But falls into abatement and low price
Even in a minute. So full of shapes is fancy
That it alone is high fantastical.

sentences.speaker: ORSINO
sentences.text:

Of what validity and pitch soe'er,
But falls into abatement and low price
Even in a minute. So full of shapes is fancy
That it alone is high fantastical.

sentences.speaker: ORSINO
sentences.text:
If music be the food of love, play on.
Give me excess of it that, surfeiting,
The appetite may sicken, and so die.
That strain again, it had a dying fall.
O, it came o'er my ear like the sweet sound,
That breathes upon a bank of violets,
Stealing and giving odor. Enough, no more.
'Tis not so sweet now as it was before.
O spirit of love, how quick and fresh art thou,
That, notwithstanding thy capacity
Receiveth as the sea, nought enters there,
Of what validity and pitch soe'er,
But falls into abatement and low price
Even in a minute. So full of shapes is fancy
That it alone is high fantastical.

sentences.speaker: CURIO
sentences.text: Will you go hunt, my lord?

sentences.speaker: ORSINO
sentences.text: What, Curio?

sentences.speaker: CURIO
sentences.text: The hart.

sentences.speaker: ORSINO
sentences.text:
Why, so I do, the noblest that I have.
Oh, when mine eyes did see Olivia first,
Methought she purged the air of pestilence.
That instant was I turned into a hart,
And my desires, like fell and cruel hounds,
E'er since pursue me.

Enter VALENTINE

sentences.speaker: ORSINO
sentences.text: How now! What news from her?

sentences.speaker: ORSINO
sentences.text: How now! What news from her?

sentences.speaker: VALENTINE
sentences.text:
So please my lord, I might not be admitted,
But from her handmaid do return this answer:
The element itself, till seven years' heat,
Shall not behold her face at ample view,
But like a cloistress, she will veiled walk
And water once a day her chamber round
With eye-offending brine —all this to season
A brother’s dead love, which she would keep fresh
And lasting in her sad remembrance.

sentences.speaker: ORSINO
sentences.text:
O, she that hath a heart of that fine frame
To pay this debt of love but to a brother,
How will she love, when the rich golden shaft
Hath killed the flock of all affections else
That live in her, when liver, brain, and heart,
These sovereign thrones, are all supplied, and filled
Her sweet perfections with one self king!
Away before me to sweet beds of flowers.
Love thoughts lie rich when canopied with bowers.

Enter VIOLA, a CAPTAIN, and sailors

sentences.speaker: VIOLA
sentences.text: What country, friends, is this?

sentences.speaker: CAPTAIN
sentences.text: This is Illyria, lady.

sentences.speaker: VIOLA
sentences.text:
And what should I do in Illyria?
My brother he is in Elysium.
Perchance he is not drown’d.—What think you, sailors?

sentences.speaker: VIOLA
sentences.text:
And what should I do in Illyria?
My brother he is in Elysium.
Perchance he is not drown’d.—What think you, sailors?

sentences.speaker: VIOLA
sentences.text:
And what should I do in Illyria?
My brother he is in Elysium.
Perchance he is not drown’d.—What think you, sailors?

sentences.speaker: VIOLA
sentences.text:
And what should I do in Illyria?
My brother he is in Elysium.
Perchance he is not drown’d.—What think you, sailors?

sentences.speaker: CAPTAIN
sentences.text: It is perchance that you yourself were saved.

sentences.speaker: VIOLA
sentences.text: O, my poor brother! And so perchance may he be.

sentences.speaker: VIOLA
sentences.text: O, my poor brother! And so perchance may he be.

sentences.speaker: CAPTAIN
sentences.text:
True, madam. And, to comfort you with chance,
Assure yourself, after our ship did split,
When you and those poor number saved with you
Hung on our driving boat, I saw your brother,
Most provident in peril, bind himself,
Courage and hope both teaching him the practice,
To a strong mast that lived upon the sea,
Where, like Arion on the dolphin’s back,
I saw him hold acquaintance with the waves
So long as I could see.

sentences.speaker: CAPTAIN

sentences.speaker: VIOLA
sentences.text:
[giving him money]
For saying so, there’s gold.
Mine own escape unfoldeth to my hope,
Whereto thy speech serves for authority,
The like of him. Know’st thou this country?

sentences.speaker: CAPTAIN
sentences.text:
Ay, madam, well, for I was bred and born
Not three hours' travel from this very place.

sentences.speaker: VIOLA
sentences.text: Who governs here?

sentences.speaker: CAPTAIN
sentences.text:
A noble duke, in nature
As in name.

sentences.speaker: VIOLA
sentences.text: What is his name?

sentences.speaker: CAPTAIN
sentences.text: Orsino.

sentences.speaker: VIOLA
sentences.text:
Orsino. I have heard my father name him.
He was a bachelor then.

sentences.speaker: VIOLA
sentences.text:
Orsino. I have heard my father name him.
He was a bachelor then.

sentences.speaker: CAPTAIN
sentences.text:
And so is now, or was so very late.
For but a month ago I went from hence,
And then ’twas fresh in murmur —as, you know,
What great ones do the less will prattle of—
That he did seek the love of fair Olivia.

sentences.speaker: CAPTAIN
sentences.text:
And so is now, or was so very late.
For but a month ago I went from hence,
And then ’twas fresh in murmur —as, you know,
What great ones do the less will prattle of—
That he did seek the love of fair Olivia.

sentences.speaker: VIOLA
sentences.text: What’s she?

sentences.speaker: CAPTAIN
sentences.text:
A virtuous maid, the daughter of a count
That died some twelvemonth since, then leaving her
In the protection of his son, her brother,
Who shortly also died, for whose dear love,
They say, she hath abjured the company
And sight of men.

sentences.speaker: CAPTAIN
sentences.text:

That died some twelvemonth since, then leaving her
In the protection of his son, her brother,
Who shortly also died, for whose dear love,
They say, she hath abjured the company
And sight of men.

sentences.speaker: CAPTAIN
sentences.text:
A virtuous maid, the daughter of a count

sentences.speaker: VIOLA
sentences.text:
Oh, that I served that lady
And might not be delivered to the world,
Till I had made mine own occasion mellow,
What my estate is.

sentences.speaker: CAPTAIN
sentences.text:
That were hard to compass,
Because she will admit no kind of suit,
No, not the duke’s.

sentences.speaker: VIOLA
sentences.text:
There is a fair behavior in thee, captain,
And though that nature with a beauteous wall
Doth oft close in pollution, yet of thee
I will believe thou hast a mind that suits
With this thy fair and outward character.
I prithee—and I’ll pay thee bounteously—
Conceal me what I am, and be my aid
For such disguise as haply shall become
The form of my intent . I’ll serve this duke.
Thou shall present me as an eunuch to him.
It may be worth thy pains, for I can sing
And speak to him in many sorts of music
That will allow me very worth his service.
What else may hap to time I will commit.
Only shape thou thy silence to my wit.

sentences.speaker: CAPTAIN
sentences.text:
Be you his eunuch, and your mute I’ll be.
When my tongue blabs, then let mine eyes not see.

sentences.speaker: CAPTAIN
sentences.text:
Be you his eunuch, and your mute I’ll be.
When my tongue blabs, then let mine eyes not see.

sentences.speaker: VIOLA
sentences.text: I thank thee. Lead me on.

sentences.speaker: VIOLA
sentences.text: I thank thee. Lead me on.
Exeunt
Enter SIR TOBY BELCH and MARIA

sentences.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
sentences.text:
What a plague means my niece, to take the death of her
brother thus? I am sure care’s an enemy to life.

sentences.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
sentences.text:
What a plague means my niece, to take the death of her
brother thus? I am sure care’s an enemy to life.

sentences.speaker: MARIA
sentences.text:
By my troth, Sir Toby, you must come in earlier o'
nights. Your cousin, my lady, takes great exceptions to
your ill hours.

sentences.speaker: MARIA
sentences.text:
By my troth, Sir Toby, you must come in earlier o'
nights. Your cousin, my lady, takes great exceptions to
your ill hours.

sentences.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
sentences.text: Why, let her except, before excepted.

sentences.speaker: MARIA
sentences.text:
Ay, but you must confine yourself within the modest
limits of order.

sentences.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
sentences.text:
Confine? I’ll confine myself no finer than I am. These
clothes are good enough to drink in, and so be these
boots too. An they be not, let them hang themselves in
their own straps.

sentences.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
sentences.text:

clothes are good enough to drink in, and so be these
boots too. An they be not, let them hang themselves in
their own straps.

sentences.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
sentences.text:

boots too. An they be not, let them hang themselves in

sentences.speaker: MARIA
sentences.text:
That quaffing and drinking will undo you: I heard my
lady talk of it yesterday, and of a foolish knight that
you brought in one night here to be her wooer.

sentences.speaker: MARIA
sentences.text:
That quaffing and drinking will undo you: I heard my
lady talk of it yesterday, and of a foolish knight that
you brought in one night here to be her wooer.

sentences.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
sentences.text: Who, Sir Andrew Aguecheek?

sentences.speaker: MARIA
sentences.text: Ay, he.

sentences.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
sentences.text: He’s as tall a man as any’s in Illyria.

sentences.speaker: MARIA
sentences.text: What’s that to the purpose?

sentences.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
sentences.text: Why, he has three thousand ducats a year.

sentences.speaker: MARIA
sentences.text:
Ay, but he’ll have but a year in all these ducats. He’s
a very fool and a prodigal.

sentences.speaker: MARIA
sentences.text:
Ay, but he’ll have but a year in all these ducats. He’s
a very fool and a prodigal.

sentences.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
sentences.text:
Fie, that you’ll say so! He plays o' the
viol-de-gamboys, and speaks three or four languages word
for word without book, and hath all the good gifts of
nature.

sentences.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
sentences.text:
Fie, that you’ll say so! He plays o' the
viol-de-gamboys, and speaks three or four languages word
for word without book, and hath all the good gifts of
nature.

sentences.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
sentences.text:
Fie, that you’ll say so! He plays o' the
viol-de-gamboys, and speaks three or four languages word
for word without book, and hath all the good gifts of
nature.

sentences.speaker: MARIA
sentences.text:
He hath indeed, almost natural, for besides that he’s a
fool, he’s a great quarreler, and but that he hath the
gift of a coward to allay the gust he hath in
quarreling, ’tis thought among the prudent he would
quickly have the gift of a grave.

sentences.speaker: MARIA

sentences.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
sentences.text:
By this hand, they are scoundrels and substractors that
say so of him. Who are they?

sentences.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
sentences.text:
By this hand, they are scoundrels and substractors that
say so of him. Who are they?

sentences.speaker: MARIA
sentences.text:
They that add, moreover, he’s drunk nightly in your
company.

sentences.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
sentences.text:
With drinking healths to my niece. I’ll drink to her as
long as there is a passage in my throat and drink in
Illyria. He’s a coward and a coistrel that will not
drink to my niece till his brains turn o' th' toe like a
parish top. What, wench! Castiliano vulgo , for here
comes Sir Andrew Agueface.

sentences.text: Sir Toby Belch! How now, Sir Toby Belch!

sentences.speaker: SIR ANDREW
sentences.text: Sir Toby Belch! How now, Sir Toby Belch!

sentences.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
sentences.text: Sweet Sir Andrew!

sentences.speaker: SIR ANDREW
sentences.text: [To MARIA] Bless you, fair shrew.

sentences.speaker: MARIA
sentences.text: And you too, sir.

sentences.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
sentences.text: Accost, Sir Andrew, accost.

sentences.speaker: SIR ANDREW
sentences.text: What’s that?

sentences.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
sentences.text: My niece’s chambermaid.

sentences.speaker: SIR ANDREW
sentences.text: Good Mistress Accost, I desire better acquaintance.

sentences.speaker: MARIA
sentences.text: My name is Mary, sir.

sentences.speaker: SIR ANDREW
sentences.text: Good Mistress Mary Accost—

sentences.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
sentences.text:
You mistake, knight. “Accost” is front her, board her,
woo her, assail her.

sentences.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
sentences.text:
You mistake, knight. “Accost” is front her, board her,
woo her, assail her.

sentences.speaker: SIR ANDREW
sentences.text:
By my troth, I would not undertake her in this company.
Is that the meaning of “accost”?

sentences.speaker: SIR ANDREW
sentences.text:
By my troth, I would not undertake her in this company.
Is that the meaning of “accost”?
Fare you well, gentlemen. [she starts to exit]

sentences.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
sentences.text:
An thou let part so, Sir Andrew, would thou mightst
never draw sword again.

sentences.speaker: SIR ANDREW
sentences.text:
An you part so, mistress, I would I might never draw
sword again. Fair lady, do you think you have fools in
hand?

sentences.speaker: SIR ANDREW
sentences.text:
An you part so, mistress, I would I might never draw
sword again. Fair lady, do you think you have fools in
hand?

sentences.speaker: MARIA
sentences.text: Sir, I have not you by the hand.

sentences.speaker: SIR ANDREW
sentences.text:
Marry, but you shall have, and here’s my hand.
[he offers her his hand]

sentences.speaker: MARIA
sentences.text:
[taking his hand] Now, sir, thought is free. I pray
you, bring your hand to the buttery-bar and let it
drink.

sentences.speaker: MARIA
sentences.text:
[taking his hand] Now, sir, thought is free. I pray
you, bring your hand to the buttery-bar and let it
drink.

sentences.speaker: SIR ANDREW
sentences.text: Wherefore, sweetheart? What’s your metaphor?

sentences.speaker: SIR ANDREW
sentences.text: Wherefore, sweetheart? What’s your metaphor?

sentences.speaker: MARIA
sentences.text: It’s dry, sir.

sentences.speaker: SIR ANDREW
sentences.text:
Why, I think so. I am not such an ass, but I can keep
my hand dry. But what’s your jest?

sentences.speaker: SIR ANDREW
sentences.text:
Why, I think so. I am not such an ass, but I can keep
my hand dry. But what’s your jest?

sentences.speaker: SIR ANDREW
sentences.text:
Why, I think so. I am not such an ass, but I can keep
my hand dry. But what’s your jest?

sentences.speaker: MARIA
sentences.text: A dry jest, sir.

sentences.speaker: SIR ANDREW
sentences.text: Are you full of them?

sentences.speaker: MARIA
sentences.text:
Ay, sir, I have them at my fingers' ends. Marry, now I
let go your hand, I am barren.

sentences.speaker: MARIA
sentences.text:
Ay, sir, I have them at my fingers' ends. Marry, now I
let go your hand, I am barren.
Exit

sentences.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
sentences.text:
O knight, thou lackest a cup of canary. When did I see
thee so put down?

sentences.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
sentences.text:
O knight, thou lackest a cup of canary. When did I see
thee so put down?

sentences.speaker: SIR ANDREW
sentences.text:
Never in your life, I think, unless you see canary put
me down. Methinks sometimes I have no more wit than a
Christian or an ordinary man has. But I am a great eater
of beef, and I believe that does harm to my wit.

sentences.speaker: SIR ANDREW
sentences.text:
Never in your life, I think, unless you see canary put
me down. Methinks sometimes I have no more wit than a
Christian or an ordinary man has. But I am a great eater
of beef, and I believe that does harm to my wit.

sentences.speaker: SIR ANDREW
sentences.text:
Never in your life, I think, unless you see canary put

Christian or an ordinary man has. But I am a great eater

sentences.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
sentences.text: No question.

sentences.speaker: SIR ANDREW
sentences.text:
An I thought that, I’d forswear it. I’ll ride home
tomorrow,
Sir Toby.

sentences.speaker: SIR ANDREW
sentences.text:
An I thought that, I’d forswear it. I’ll ride home
tomorrow,
Sir Toby.

sentences.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
sentences.text: Pourquoi, my dear knight?

sentences.speaker: SIR ANDREW
sentences.text:
What is “pourquoi”? Do, or not do? I would I had
bestowed that time in the tongues that I have in
fencing, dancing, and bear-baiting. O, had I but
followed the arts!

sentences.speaker: SIR ANDREW
sentences.text:
What is “pourquoi”? Do, or not do? I would I had
bestowed that time in the tongues that I have in
fencing, dancing, and bear-baiting. O, had I but
followed the arts!

sentences.text:
What is “pourquoi”? Do, or not do? I would I had

sentences.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
sentences.text: Then hadst thou had an excellent head of hair.

sentences.speaker: SIR ANDREW
sentences.text: Why, would that have mended my hair?

sentences.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
sentences.text:
Past question, for thou seest it will not curl by
nature.

sentences.speaker: SIR ANDREW
sentences.text: But it becomes me well enough, does ’t not?

sentences.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
sentences.text:
Excellent. It hangs like flax on a distaff. And I hope
to see a housewife take thee between her legs and spin
it off.

sentences.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
sentences.text:
Excellent. It hangs like flax on a distaff. And I hope
to see a housewife take thee between her legs and spin
it off.

sentences.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
sentences.text:
Excellent. It hangs like flax on a distaff. And I hope
to see a housewife take thee between her legs and spin
it off.

sentences.speaker: SIR ANDREW
sentences.text:
Faith, I’ll home tomorrow, Sir Toby. Your niece will
not be seen. Or if she be, it’s four to one she’ll none
of me. The count himself here hard by woos her.

sentences.speaker: SIR ANDREW
sentences.text:
Faith, I’ll home tomorrow, Sir Toby. Your niece will
not be seen. Or if she be, it’s four to one she’ll none
of me. The count himself here hard by woos her.

sentences.speaker: SIR ANDREW
sentences.text:

sentences.speaker: SIR ANDREW
sentences.text:
Faith, I’ll home tomorrow, Sir Toby. Your niece will
not be seen. Or if she be, it’s four to one she’ll none
of me. The count himself here hard by woos her.

sentences.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
sentences.text:
She’ll none o' the count. She’ll not match above her
degree, neither in estate, years, nor wit. I have heard
her swear ’t . Tut, there’s life in ’t, man.

sentences.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
sentences.text:
She’ll none o' the count. She’ll not match above her
degree, neither in estate, years, nor wit. I have heard
her swear ’t . Tut, there’s life in ’t, man.

sentences.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
sentences.text:
She’ll none o' the count. She’ll not match above her
degree, neither in estate, years, nor wit. I have heard
her swear ’t . Tut, there’s life in ’t, man.

sentences.speaker: SIR ANDREW
sentences.text:
I’ll stay a month longer. I am a fellow o' th'
strangest mind i' th' world. I delight in masques and
revels sometimes altogether.

sentences.speaker: SIR ANDREW
sentences.text:
I’ll stay a month longer. I am a fellow o' th'
strangest mind i' th' world. I delight in masques and
revels sometimes altogether.

sentences.speaker: SIR ANDREW

strangest mind i' th' world. I delight in masques and

sentences.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
sentences.text: Art thou good at these kickshawses, knight?

sentences.speaker: SIR ANDREW
sentences.text:
As any man in Illyria, whatsoever he be, under the
degree of my betters. And yet I will not compare with an
old man.

sentences.speaker: SIR ANDREW
sentences.text:
As any man in Illyria, whatsoever he be, under the
degree of my betters. And yet I will not compare with an
old man.

sentences.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
sentences.text: What is thy excellence in a galliard, knight?

sentences.speaker: SIR ANDREW
sentences.text: Faith, I can cut a caper.

sentences.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
sentences.text: And I can cut the mutton to ’t.

sentences.speaker: SIR ANDREW
sentences.text:
And I think I have the back-trick simply as strong as
any man in Illyria.

sentences.speaker: SIR TOBY BELCH
sentences.text:
Wherefore are these things hid? Wherefore have these
gifts a curtain before 'em? Are they like to take dust,
like Mistress Mall’s picture? Why dost thou not go to
church in a galliard and come home in a coranto? My very
walk should be a jig. I would not so much as make water
but in a sink-a-pace. What dost thou mean? Is it a
world to hide virtues in? I did think, by the excellent
constitution of thy leg, it was formed under the star of
a galliard.
